source.name: Archaeologist
source.description: Successfully investigate an archaeological site.
source.requirement: Complete all levels of an archaeological site
source.hint: The Galactic Doorstep origin will create an archaeological site in the starting system within the first years of the game.

conditions.notes: Requires completing any archaeological site; starting conditions usually do not hard-block it.

curation.planner_notes: Good baseline exploration goal; Galactic Doorstep can make a site appear early.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count completed archaeology sites.

source.name: Brave New World
source.description: Colonize a planet.
source.requirement: Number of owned planets > 1
source.hint: Starting the game with the Guaranteed Habitable Worlds setting to 1 or 2 creates very suitable planets nearby.

conditions.notes: Requires colonizing beyond the capital.

curation.planner_notes: Usually natural in any non-challenge run; guaranteed habitable worlds speed it up.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count owned planets.

source.name: Break On Through...
source.description: Research a Rare technology.
source.requirement: Number of acquired rare techs > 0
source.hint: The Void Dwellers and Teachers of the Shroud origins start with a rare technology as a permanent research option.

conditions.notes: Requires acquiring at least one rare technology.

curation.planner_notes: Teachers of the Shroud and Void Dwellers can make rare tech access easier.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count acquired rare technologies.

source.name: Domo Arigato
source.description: Build a Robot Pop.
source.requirement: Build a robot pop
source.hint: The Mechanist origin starts with the technology to build robots.

conditions.notes: Requires building a robot pop.

warnings: Empire policies, technologies, or ethics can delay robot access.
curation.planner_notes: Mechanist starts with robot-building support.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet identify built robot pops.

source.name: Emissary
source.description: Explore a Natural Wormhole.
source.requirement: Travel through any wormhole.
source.hint: Wormhole travel requires the Wormhole Stabilization technology. Start the game with the Wormhole Pairs setting to 5x.

conditions.notes: Requires traveling through a wormhole after stabilizing wormhole travel.

warnings: Low wormhole density can make this slower.
curation.planner_notes: Higher Wormhole Pairs galaxy setting makes this easier.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect wormhole traversal history.

source.name: Energetic
source.description: Store/have 1000 EC.
source.requirement: Have at least 1000 energy in storage
source.hint: This achievement will be earned naturally while playing the game.

conditions.notes: Requires 1000 stored energy credits.

curation.planner_notes: Naturally earned during normal expansion; no special setup needed.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet read stored energy credit amounts.

source.name: Explorer
source.description: Survey one of each basic planet-class.
source.requirement: Survey all 16 celestial body types
source.hint: This achievement will be earned naturally while playing the game.

conditions.notes: Requires surveying at least one planet.

curation.planner_notes: Natural in the opening exploration loop.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count surveyed planets.

source.name: Mutual Understanding
source.description: Successfully negotiate a trade deal.
source.requirement: Trade with another empire
source.hint: If no empire wants to trade, gifts count too.

conditions.notes: Any trade deal with another empire, including gifts, triggers the achievement.

curation.planner_notes: Gifts count as trade — send a small gift of resources to the first empire you meet.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track trade deal history.

source.name: Resourceful
source.description: Have access to ten different strategic resources.
source.requirement: income >= 1.
source.hint: Need to have >=1 income for all 11 tradable resources. Achievement tooltip is out of date.

conditions.notes: Requires monthly income >= 1 from at least 10 different strategic resource types.

warnings: The actual in-game tooltip requirement may be out of date; the wiki states >=1 income for all 11 tradable strategic resources.
curation.planner_notes: Trade enclaves, vassal taxation, and galactic market purchases can supplement strategic resource income if deposits are scarce.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet read individual strategic resource income values.

source.name: Rise of the Machines
source.description: As a Determined Exterminator, conquer or eliminate all biological Empires in the galaxy.
source.requirement: As a Determined Exterminator, have the number of organic empires be < 1
source.hint: Curiously, the Determined Exterminator civic is not required to complete this achievement.

conditions.notes: The achievement fires when no biological empires remain; the wiki hint states the Determined Exterminator civic is not actually required despite the achievement description.

warnings: The achievement description mentions Determined Exterminator, but the wiki records a special-case oversight where the civic is not required.
Galaxy generation must include biological empires or targets to eliminate.
curation.planner_notes: Use the wiki hint as source of truth: any empire that can reduce biological empires below one can satisfy this, though Determined Exterminator remains the thematic and practical route.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count remaining biological empires.

source.name: Suffer not the Alien
source.description: As a xenophobic empire, purge all other sentient species in the galaxy.
source.requirement: As a xenophobe empire, purge all sapient alien species in the galaxy.
source.hint: Alien pops in your empire will be automatically purged if you have the Fanatic Purifiers civic.

conditions.notes: Requires Xenophobe or Fanatic Xenophobe ethic.
Requires all sapient alien species to be purged from the galaxy.

warnings: Galaxy generation must include at least one alien species to purge.
curation.planner_notes: Fanatic Purifiers civic provides automatic purging and makes this easier. The setup locks other ethic-dependent achievements.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads ethics but does not yet count purged populations or detect all-species-eliminated state.

source.name: They Come In Pieces
source.description: Vivisect an alien.
source.requirement: Vivisect an alien during First Contact
source.hint: The option to vivisect aliens requires the Aggressive First Contact Protocol policy, and is not available to all ethics.

conditions.notes: Requires the Aggressive First Contact Protocol policy and selecting the vivisection option.

warnings: Not available to all ethics; some pacifist or xenophile ethics block the Aggressive First Contact Protocol.
curation.planner_notes: Set empire policy to Aggressive First Contact Protocol. The vivisection option appears when investigating a pre-FTL civilization with that policy active.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet identify First Contact protocol policies or event outcomes.

source.name: We Come In Peace
source.description: Complete a mutually friendly First Contact with an empire with no negative incidents.
source.requirement: Have no negative events happen to you or another empire during First Contact.
source.hint: The player has to have the Proactive or Cautious First Contact Protocol policy to get the achievement.

conditions.notes: Requires Proactive or Cautious First Contact Protocol and no negative incidents during the First Contact phase.

warnings: Negative First Contact events are partially RNG-dependent; Aggressive Protocol is incompatible.
curation.planner_notes: Use Proactive or Cautious First Contact Protocol. Avoid hostile actions that may trigger negative incidents before contact is fully established.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track First Contact incident history.

source.name: A Home Away From Home
source.description: Conquer another species homeworld.
source.requirement: Conquer another species' homeworld
source.hint: Pre-FTL species count for this achievement.

conditions.notes: Requires conquering the homeworld of any sapient species including pre-FTLs.

curation.planner_notes: Pre-FTL homeworlds count and are easier targets; high Pre-FTL galaxy settings increase odds of finding one.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet identify planetary homeworld origin or conquest flags.

source.name: Battle Thralls
source.description: Have 3 other Empires as vassals.
source.requirement: Number of vassals > 2
source.hint: Creating 3 sectors and releasing them as vassals may be faster than forcefully vassalizing empires.

conditions.notes: Requires at least 3 subject empires (tributaries, vassals, protectorates, or specialized subjects).

curation.planner_notes: Releasing sectors as vassals is faster than forceful vassalization through war. The Domination tradition tree helps.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count vassalized empires.

source.name: Beyond the Veil
source.description: Breach the Shroud.
source.requirement: Take the Breach the Shroud tradition and complete the resulting special project.
source.hint: Empires with the Teachers of the Shroud origin can get this achievement faster.

conditions.notes: Must have completed the Psionic ascension path (mutually exclusive with other paths).
Requires completing the special project that follows the Breach the Shroud tradition.

warnings: Requires Psionic Theory technology and the Transcendence ascension perk; spiritualist ethics make this easier.
curation.planner_notes: Start with Teachers of the Shroud origin (Overlord DLC) for a guaranteed early path to psionic theory.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect completed ascension paths or special projects.

source.name: Building Better Worlds
source.description: Terraform a planet.
source.requirement: Terraform a planet
source.hint: This achievement can be obtained once you research Terrestrial Sculpting.

conditions.notes: Requires terraforming at least one planet.

warnings: Requires the Terrestrial Sculpting technology.
curation.planner_notes: Research Terrestrial Sculpting (Society tech, appears mid-game) and terraform any colonizable planet.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect terraforming completion.

source.name: Clever Girl
source.description: Uplift a species.
source.requirement: Uplift a species
source.hint: Start a game with Pre-FTL Civilizations and Pre-Sapient Species set to max.

conditions.notes: Requires uplifting a pre-sapient species to full sapience.

warnings: Requires pre-sapient species in the galaxy; high galaxy settings for pre-sapients improve availability.
curation.planner_notes: Increase Pre-Sapient Species galaxy setting. The Epigenetic Triggers technology enables uplifting.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect species uplift completion.

source.name: Controlled Evolution
source.description: Genetically modify a species to possess traits worth 7 points.
source.requirement: Modify a species which has > 7 trait points
source.hint: Requires the Engineered Evolution ascension perk and completing the Genetics tradition tree.

conditions.notes: Requires modifying a species with more than 7 total trait point value.
Engineered Evolution ascension perk is required to exceed 7 trait points.

warnings: Engineered Evolution ascension perk requires completing Genetics tradition tree.
curation.planner_notes: Prioritize Genetics tradition tree to unlock Engineered Evolution, then use gene-modding on a species with existing positive traits to push past 7 points.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect species trait points or genetic modification outcomes.

source.name: Digging Deep
source.description: Earn a total Mineral income each month above 250.
source.requirement: Monthly income of minerals > 249
source.hint: This achievement will be earned naturally while playing the game.

conditions.notes: Requires monthly mineral income > 249.

curation.planner_notes: Naturally reached as colonies develop mining districts; arc furnace megastructures are an alternative source in the Machine Age DLC.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet read monthly resource income values.

source.name: Distinctiveness Added
source.description: As a Driven Assimilator, own cyborg Pops of at least 5 different species.
source.requirement: As a Driven Assimilator, have 5 different cyborg species
source.hint: Start the game with the Pre-FTL Civilizations and Pre-Sapient Species set to max. Invade and assimilate 4 civilizations.

conditions.notes: Requires the Driven Assimilator civic (Machine Intelligence authority).
Requires assimilating at least 5 different species into cyborgs.

warnings: Galaxy must contain enough diverse species to reach 5 different cyborg species.
curation.planner_notes: Set Pre-FTL and Pre-Sapient to max. Assimilate multiple species aggressively.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count cyborg species by origin.

source.name: League of Nations
source.description: Be a founding member of the Galactic Community.
source.requirement: Choose to support the Galactic Community when it is first proposed
source.hint: The Common Ground and Hegemon origins guarantee this achievement unless another empire has the same origins.

conditions.notes: Requires voting to support the Galactic Community when it is first proposed. If passed without your support or rejected, this cannot be obtained.

warnings: If the initial GC proposal passes without the player empire as a supporter, the achievement is permanently missed.
curation.planner_notes: Common Ground and Hegemon origins guarantee this achievement unless another empire with the same origin proposes it first.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect Galactic Community founding membership.

source.name: Like Tears in Rain
source.description: Evolve into perfect, immortal machines.
source.requirement: Complete Synthetic ascension as a non-Machine empire.
source.hint: Easiest to achieve with the Synthetic Fertility origin.

conditions.notes: Must complete the Synthetic Evolution ascension path (mutually exclusive with other paths).

warnings: Cannot be completed as a Machine Intelligence empire — must be a biological non-gestalt or Hive Mind empire that transitions into synthetics.
curation.planner_notes: Synthetic Fertility origin makes this easier by starting with cybernetic tech progress.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect completed ascension paths.

source.name: Megapolis
source.description: Have a planet with at least 10,000 Pops.
source.requirement: Have a planet with > 9,999 Pops.
source.hint: This achievement will be earned naturally while playing the game.

conditions.notes: Requires a single planet with > 9999 pops.

warnings: Requires extremely high population on a single planet; habitat or ring world segments may overflow more easily.
curation.planner_notes: A Void Dweller habitat or a ring world segment with multiple housing districts, high habitability, and pop growth buffs is the most reliable approach.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet read per-planet population counts.

source.name: New Shining Star
source.description: Upgrade your capital to an Imperial Complex.
source.requirement: Upgrade your homeworld's capital building to System-Capital Complex
source.hint: This achievement will be earned naturally while playing the game.

conditions.notes: Requires upgrading the capital building to Imperial Complex / System-Capital Complex.

curation.planner_notes: Naturally earned as the capital planet develops; no special setup required.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet read building levels on the capital.

source.name: Old Friends
source.description: Receive a gift from a Fallen Empire.
source.requirement: Accept a gift from a patronising Enigmatic Observer or Keepers of Knowledge Fallen Empire
source.hint: Accepting a request or completing a task will eventually lead to a gift being bestowed.

conditions.notes: Requires completing requests or tasks for an Enigmatic Observer or Keepers of Knowledge Fallen Empire until a gift is bestowed.

warnings: Only Enigmatic Observer (xenophile) and Keepers of Knowledge (materialist) Fallen Empires give gifts; other types (xenophobe/spiritualist) do not.
Gift events are not guaranteed in every interaction chain.
curation.planner_notes: Increase Fallen Empire count in galaxy setup to improve the odds of a gift-giving type spawning. Complete their requests as they appear.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track Fallen Empire interaction outcomes.

source.name: Omnicultural
source.description: Have your ruler be of a different species class than your founding species.
source.requirement: The ruler of your empire is different from your founding species
source.hint: Oligarchic and Corporate authority changes rulers often and allows the manual selection of a ruler for 2000 Unity.

conditions.notes: Requires a ruler whose species class differs from the empire's founding species. Oligarchic and Corporate authorities cycle rulers frequently.

warnings: Dictatorial and Imperial authorities only change rulers on death, making this harder.
curation.planner_notes: Choose Oligarchic or Corporate authority for frequent ruler turnover. The manual ruler selection costs 2000 Unity.
known_limitations: The current save parser can read the current ruler species but does not yet compare it against the founding species.

source.name: Payback
source.description: As a humanoid species, infiltrate the homeworld of pre-FTL reptilians.
source.requirement: As a humanoid species, complete Covert Infiltration of a primitive reptilian species and incorporate them into the empire
source.hint: Setting Habitable Worlds to 0.25x and Pre-FTL Civilizations and Pre-Sapient Species to 5x ensures a reptilian civilization is found more easily.

conditions.notes: Player founding species must be Humanoid.
Requires finding a pre-FTL reptilian homeworld to infiltrate.
Requires completing covert infiltration and incorporation.

warnings: Depends on galaxy generation or manually high Pre-FTL settings.
curation.planner_notes: Start as Humanoid and raise Pre-FTL density to improve odds.
known_limitations: The current save parser can read species class but does not yet identify pre-FTL target species.

source.name: Peacekeeper
source.description: As a pacifistic empire, be at peace for 200 consecutive years (crises do not count as wars).
source.requirement: Don't go to war for 200 consecutive years as a pacifist empire
source.hint: Use the zero empires trick and no pre-FTL species and play for 200 years.

conditions.notes: Requires a pacifist empire.
Requires 200 consecutive peaceful years; wars reset progress.

warnings: Unexpected wars can reset the timer; crises do not count as wars for this achievement.
curation.planner_notes: Zero-empires setup reduces interruption risk.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads ethics but does not yet track continuous peace duration.

source.name: Power Overwhelming
source.description: Store/have 5000 EC.
source.requirement: Accumulate energy credits > 4999
source.hint: This achievement will be earned naturally while playing the game.

conditions.notes: Requires more than 5000 stored energy credits.

curation.planner_notes: Naturally earned as the economy scales; Dyson Sphere or trade value strategies accelerate this.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet read stored energy credit amounts.

source.name: Supremacy
source.description: Win a War against another Empire.
source.requirement: Win a war.
source.hint: Must be the empire that declared war or was declared war against.

conditions.notes: Requires winning at least one war.

warnings: Rival empires that refuse to surrender can delay war conclusion.
curation.planner_notes: Nearly automatic in any run that prosecutes at least one war.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track war outcomes.

source.name: The Grand Fleet
source.description: Build a fleet with a total fleet-size above 500.
source.requirement: Have a fleet with size > 500
source.hint: See Command Limit on how to increase the Command Limit.

conditions.notes: Requires a single fleet exceeding 500 command limit (not total naval capacity).

warnings: Requires high command limit from technology, traditions, and officer council positions.
curation.planner_notes: Focus on technologies and traditions that increase command limit; Titan and Juggernaut bonuses help.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet inspect individual fleet command limit values.

source.name: Tradition is Everything
source.description: Unlock all 42 Traditions.
source.requirement: Unlock all 42 Traditions
source.hint: Start with the Tech/Tradition Cost setting to 0.25.

conditions.notes: All 42 tradition picks must be unlocked across the 7 tradition trees and 2 ascension perk slots per tree.

curation.planner_notes: Set Tech/Tradition Cost to 0.25x in galaxy setup to significantly speed up tradition adoption. The Harmony finisher reduces tradition cost. Moderate-difficulty empire sprawl management helps.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count adopted traditions.

source.name: View from the End of the World
source.description: Build a Habitat station in a Black Hole system.
source.requirement: Have an owned Habitat in a system with a black hole
source.hint: The achievement does not trigger on stars that were turned into black holes by events.

conditions.notes: Requires building a Habitat.
The Habitat must be built in a system with a naturally occurring black hole. Black holes created by event chains do not count.

warnings: The black hole must be a naturally spawned one, not created by events such as the Horizon Signal chain.
curation.planner_notes: Survey systems near your starting area to find a natural black hole, then build a Habitat there once the Habitat technology (Utopia DLC content) is available.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect Habitat ownership or distinguish black hole types.

source.name: We Are Legion
source.description: Be a biological Hive Mind with at least 100,000 drone Pops.
source.requirement: Be a Hive Mind with > 99,999 Pops.
source.hint: This achievement can be obtained surprisingly fast by playing with Logistic Growth Ceiling to maximum.

conditions.notes: Must be a Hive Mind empire (gestalt).
Requires over 99,999 drone pops.

warnings: Very high population threshold; only achievable in very long or maximized-growth games.
curation.planner_notes: Set Logistic Growth Ceiling to maximum and prioritize pop growth and assembly. Habitat spam can help generate more pop growth locations.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count total pops.

source.name: ... To The Other Side
source.description: Research 15 Rare technologies in a single game.
source.requirement: Number of acquired rare techs > 14
source.hint: The Void Dwellers and Teachers of the Shroud origins start with a rare technology as a permanent research option.

conditions.notes: Requires acquiring at least 15 rare technologies in a single playthrough.

warnings: Rare technology draw is subject to RNG; some rare techs require specific prerequisites.
curation.planner_notes: Void Dwellers and Teachers of the Shroud origins start with a permanent rare tech research option, making progression easier. Set Tech Cost to minimum and prioritise rare-tech-draw physics and society alternatives.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count acquired rare technologies.

source.name: Birth of a Federation
source.description: Establish a Federation.
source.requirement: Be the leader of a federation.
source.hint: Empires with the Fanatical Befrienders personality offer the highest acceptance rate.

conditions.notes: Requires forming or joining a federation and becoming the federation president.

curation.planner_notes: Improve relations with nearby empires and propose a federation. The Common Ground origin guarantees a federation at game start.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect federation membership or leadership.

source.name: Captive Star
source.description: Construct a complete Dyson Sphere.
source.requirement: Construct a complete Dyson Sphere
source.hint: Repairing a destroyed Dyson Sphere does not count.

conditions.notes: Requires building a Dyson Sphere to completion. Repairing a pre-existing Dyson Sphere does not trigger this achievement.

warnings: The achievement specifically requires new construction; repairing a damaged Dyson Sphere does not count.
curation.planner_notes: Requires Mega-Engineering technology and significant alloy/influence investment. Galactic Doorstep or a ruined Dyson Sphere discovery can help locate a suitable star.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect completed megastructures or distinguish new construction from repairs.

source.name: Center of Trade
source.description: Earn at least 1000 Energy Credits per month from trade.
source.requirement: Earn > 999 energy credits from your trade policy.
source.hint: Wealth Creation trade policy needs 1000 Trade Value, all others require 2000.

conditions.notes: Requires > 999 energy credits per month from trade policy conversion. Effective trade value needed depends on the chosen trade policy: ~1000 for Wealth Creation, ~2000 for other policies.

warnings: The trade policy in use affects the raw trade value needed; Wealth Creation (1:1 conversion) is the most efficient for this achievement.
curation.planner_notes: Build a trade-focused empire with a Commercial Pact or a Trading Federation; branch offices on the Mercantile tradition or Thrifty species trait help.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet read trade policy or monthly energy-from-trade values.

source.name: Citadel of Death
source.description: Own a Citadel with 40k fleet power.
source.requirement: Have a Citadel with > 39999 fleet power
source.hint: Upgrade a starbase to a Citadel and then develop it into a Bastion.

conditions.notes: Requires a Citadel-class starbase with >39,999 fleet power.

warnings: Starbase command limit techs, anchorage modules, and naval logistics buildings are needed.
curation.planner_notes: Upgrade a key choke-point starbase to Citadel and stack anchorage + defensive modules.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet inspect individual starbase fleet power values.

source.name: Enlightened Times
source.description: Enlighten a Bronze or Stone age civilization.
source.requirement: Enlighten a primitive civilization from the Bronze or Stone Age
source.hint: Setting Habitable Worlds to 0.25x and Pre-FTL Civilizations and Pre-Sapient Species to 5x ensures a civilization with the correct era is found more easily.

conditions.notes: A Bronze Age or Stone Age primitive civilization must exist as a valid target.
Enlightenment is the peaceful path — raising the civilization to the space age.

warnings: The required primitive era may not spawn; high Pre-FTL galaxy settings improve odds.
curation.planner_notes: Set Pre-FTL Civilizations to 5x and Habitable Worlds to 0.25x to find early-era primitives faster.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet classify pre-FTL era or track enlightenment completion.

source.name: Faster, Stronger, Better
source.description: Genetically alter a species (not uplift).
source.requirement: Genetically modify a species
source.hint: Can be obtained from the start if the Toxoids DLC is installed via the Overtuned origin.

conditions.notes: Requires applying any genetic modification template to a species.

warnings: Requires Gene Tailoring technology (or start with Overtuned origin from Toxoids DLC).
curation.planner_notes: Research Gene Tailoring and modify any species with at least one trait change. The Overtuned origin (Toxoids DLC) starts with the ability to gene-mod immediately.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect species genetic modification.

source.name: Grand Admiral
source.description: Empire total fleet military power equals 100000.
source.requirement: have fleet power > 99999
source.hint: This achievement will be earned naturally while playing the game for a reasonable amount of time.

conditions.notes: Requires total fleet power exceeding 99,999.

curation.planner_notes: Natural in any mid-to-late-game economy; focus on alloys and naval capacity.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet sum fleet power from all active fleets.

source.name: I Can See Forever
source.description: Construct a complete Sentry Array.
source.requirement: Construct a complete Sentry Array
source.hint: Repairing a destroyed Sentry Array does not count.

conditions.notes: Requires building a Sentry Array to completion. Repairing a pre-existing Sentry Array does not trigger the achievement.

warnings: New construction is required; repairing a ruined Sentry Array does not count.
curation.planner_notes: Requires Mega-Engineering. The Sentry Array provides full-galaxy sensor coverage and is the cheapest of the original megastructures to build.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect completed megastructures or distinguish new construction from repairs.

source.name: Imperial Highway
source.description: Own 4 active Gateways.
source.requirement: Own 4 Gateways which have been activated
source.hint: Start a game with the Abandoned Gateways setting to 5x and the Galactic Doorstep origin.

conditions.notes: Requires owning 4 activated gateways.

warnings: Gateway activation requires the Gateway Restoration technology.
curation.planner_notes: Set Abandoned Gateways to 5x and consider the Galactic Doorstep origin to maximize gateway availability. Alternatively, build new gateways once the technology is researched.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count owned gateways.

source.name: It belongs in a museum!
source.description: Find a Relic.
source.requirement: Own at least 1 relic
source.hint: Start a game with the Treasure Hunters origin.

conditions.notes: Requires owning at least one relic from any source.

curation.planner_notes: Completing an archaeological site or starting with the Treasure Hunters origin (Grand Archive DLC) provides a reliable relic.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet identify owned relic count.

source.name: Opposites Attract
source.description: Have all eight ethics represented within your Federation.
source.requirement: Have empires representing every ethic in your Federation
source.hint: It is easier for the player's empire to change their own ethics when already in a federation with five unique ethics from other empires.

conditions.notes: Requires all eight ethics (fanatic variants count as their base) present across federation members.

warnings: Requires finding or developing empires that collectively cover all eight ethics; the player may need to shift ethics late-game.
curation.planner_notes: Build a large, diverse federation. If short one ethic, the player's empire can shift ethics using faction influence or the Reform Government agenda.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track federation member ethics.

source.name: Paradise Found
source.description: Terraform a planet into a Gaia world.
source.requirement: Terraform a planet into a Gaia World
source.hint: Gaia Worlds created by events also count.

conditions.notes: Requires creating (via terraforming or event) a Gaia world that is owned by the player.

warnings: The World Shaper ascension perk is required for deliberate Gaia terraforming. Event-based Gaia worlds (e.g., the Baol precursor chain) also count.
curation.planner_notes: Take the World Shaper ascension perk and terraform any planet, or rely on the Baol precursor chain to create Gaia worlds via the Active Relic action.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count owned planets by class or detect terraforming outcomes.

source.name: Planet of the Mechs
source.description: Terraform a planet into a Machine World.
source.requirement: Terraform a planet into a Machine World
source.hint: Two ascension perks are required to be chosen before the option to unlock Machine Worlds is made available.

conditions.notes: Requires terraforming an owned planet into a Machine World.
The Machine Worlds ascension perk requires two prior perks and is itself the third.

warnings: Only machine empires or empires that have completed Synthetic ascension can use Machine Worlds; organic empires do not benefit from habitability on Machine Worlds.
curation.planner_notes: A Machine Intelligence empire can take the Machine Worlds perk as the third ascension perk and terraform any non-Machine World planet.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect planet class changes after terraforming.

source.name: Retirement Home
source.description: As a Rogue Servitor, own at least 1000 Pops from Fallen Empires.
source.requirement: Have the Rogue Servitor civic and > 999 pops which started the game in a Fallen Empire
source.hint: The 1000 pops required for the achievement cannot be gestalts.

conditions.notes: Requires the Rogue Servitor civic (Machine Intelligence authority).
Requires at least 1,000 pops that originated in a Fallen Empire.

warnings: Requires conquest or assimilation of Fallen Empire pops. FE must awaken or be attacked.
The 1,000 pops must be non-gestalt biologicals (can't be gestalt pops).
curation.planner_notes: Conquer/integrate Fallen Empire planets and relocate their bio-trophy pops. Late-game FE war is required.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads civics but does not yet count pops by their original empire.

source.name: Ringworld Engineers
source.description: Construct a complete Ring World.
source.requirement: Construct a complete Ring World megastructure
source.hint: Repairing a destroyed Ringworld does not count.

conditions.notes: Requires building a Ring World to completion. Repairing a ruined Ring World does not trigger the achievement.

warnings: New construction is required; repairing a ruined Ring World does not count.
curation.planner_notes: Extremely expensive in alloys and influence. The Cybrex precursor chain provides a ruined Ring World, but repairing it does not qualify — new construction is needed.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect completed megastructures or distinguish new construction from repairs.

source.name: Slave to the Systems
source.description: As an authoritarian Empire with at least 50,000 Pops, have at least 20,000 enslaved Pops.
source.requirement: Have total Pops > 49,999 and Enslaved Pops > 19,999
source.hint: The Slaver Guilds or Indentured Assets civic will ensure the correct ratio if you have 50,000 Pops.

conditions.notes: Requires authoritarian ethic.
Requires total empire population > 49,999.
Requires > 19,999 enslaved pops within the empire.

warnings: Ethic drift away from authoritarian could eventually invalidate the setup requirement.
curation.planner_notes: Slaver Guilds or Indentured Assets civics help maintain a high enslaved ratio as population grows.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads ethics but does not yet count enslaved pops.

source.name: Star Struck
source.description: Own 200 Starbases (Outposts are counted).
source.requirement: Have 200 Starbases including Outposts
source.hint: This achievement will be earned naturally while playing the game for a reasonable amount of time.

conditions.notes: Requires owning at least 200 starbases (outposts count toward this total).

warnings: Each colonized system contributes one outpost; war and claims are needed to reach 200 in a standard-size galaxy.
curation.planner_notes: Expand aggressively and claim systems; habitats and gateway systems also increase the starbase count.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count owned starbases or outposts.

source.name: Strange Mood
source.description: As a Master Crafter empire, fully construct a megastructure while you have a Covenant with a Shroud entity.
source.requirement: As a Masterful Crafters empire, fully construct a megastructure while you have a Covenant with a Shroud entity.
source.hint: The megastructure only needs to reach full completion while the covenant is active.

conditions.notes: Requires the Masterful Crafters civic.
Any megastructure fully constructed qualifies.
Must have an active Covenant with a Shroud entity at the moment of completion.

warnings: Requires psionic ascension or other path to Shroud access, which may conflict with other civics/ethics.
Covenant can be lost if the Shroud entity is displeased.
curation.planner_notes: Combine megastructure construction and psionic ascension in one run. Time the completion to occur while the covenant is active.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads civics but does not yet detect megastructure completion or covenant state.

source.name: The Industrial Re-Revolution
source.description: Earn a total Mineral income each month above 1000.
source.requirement: Monthly income of minerals > 999
source.hint: This achievement will be earned naturally while playing the game for a reasonable amount of time.

conditions.notes: Requires monthly mineral income > 999.

curation.planner_notes: Heavy mineral world specialization, orbital rings with mineral processing, or an Arc Furnace megastructure can push income past 1000.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet read monthly resource income values.

source.name: Think Tank
source.description: Construct a complete Science Nexus.
source.requirement: Construct a complete Science Nexus
source.hint: Repairing a destroyed Science Nexus does not count.

conditions.notes: Requires building a Science Nexus to completion. Repairing a ruined Science Nexus does not trigger the achievement.

warnings: New construction is required; repairing a ruined Science Nexus does not count.
curation.planner_notes: The cheapest of the original megastructures to build; still requires Mega-Engineering and substantial alloy stockpiles.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect completed megastructures or distinguish new construction from repairs.

source.name: Towards Utopia
source.description: As an Egalitarian empire, have at least 50,000 pops living under Utopian living conditions in your empire.
source.requirement: Have > 49,999 pops under Utopian living standards

conditions.notes: Requires unlocking any ascension perk.

curation.planner_notes: Natural after completing a tradition tree.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count ascension perks.

source.name: Unlimited Power!
source.description: Use the active effect of a Relic.
source.requirement: Use the active effect of a Relic by spending Unity or other resources
source.hint: Start a game with the Treasure Hunters origin and complete the relic.

conditions.notes: Requires activating the active ability of any relic (costs Unity or other resources).

curation.planner_notes: Start with the Treasure Hunters origin for a guaranteed early relic, or obtain relics through normal gameplay.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track relic active-effect usage.

source.name: Victorious
source.description: Win the game through any victory condition in Ironman Mode.
source.requirement: Finish the game in Ironman mode via one of the victory conditions
source.hint: Set the mid-game, end-game, victory years to minimum.

conditions.notes: Requires reaching a victory year score victory (or another victory condition such as Crisis ascension) in an Ironman game.

warnings: Ironman mode is required; non-Ironman games do not qualify.
curation.planner_notes: Set mid-game, end-game, and victory years to their minimum values for the fastest completion. The score victory is the most common path.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect victory conditions or game-over state.

source.name: What Came Before
source.description: Find the home system of a precursor empire.
source.requirement: Travel to the home system of a precursor empire
source.hint: Precursor homeworlds are revealed by completing a precursor event chain.

conditions.notes: Completing any precursor chain reveals the precursor home system.

warnings: Precursor assignment is RNG-based; forcing a specific precursor via galaxy settings is not possible in the base game.
curation.planner_notes: Send science ships to survey many systems to find precursor archaeology sites faster.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect precursor chain completion or home system travel.

source.name: Xenophage
source.description: Keep at least 5 different species as livestock in your Empire.
source.requirement: Have > 4 species enslaved as Livestock.
source.hint: Start the game with Pre-FTL Civilizations and Pre-Sapient Species set to max. Enslave 5 of them as Livestock.

conditions.notes: Requires more than four different species enslaved as Livestock.
Empire must be able to set species rights to Livestock.

warnings: Requires species-rights support for Livestock; empire ethics/civics may block this.
curation.planner_notes: High Pre-FTL and pre-sapient settings help source enough species.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count species rights or livestock species.

source.name: All-Seeing Eye
source.description: Have an Intel Level of 100 on five different empires simultaneously.
source.requirement: Have 100 Intel on five different empires at the same time.
source.hint: The Sentry Array increases your passive Intel level by 40 each when fully upgraded, and the value stacks.

conditions.notes: Requires full intel (level 100) on five empires at the same time.

warnings: A fully-upgraded Sentry Array provides +40 passive intel per array; multiple arrays stack.
curation.planner_notes: Building a Sentry Array dramatically simplifies this; otherwise rely on espionage networks and codebreaking.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track per-empire intel levels.

source.name: Giga-Engineering
source.description: Have at least 4 fully operational Megastructures within your borders (Habitats, Ring Worlds and Gateways do not count).
source.requirement: Have 4 completed Megastructures.
source.hint: Repairing destroyed Megastructures also count.

conditions.notes: Requires 4 completed megastructures excluding habitats, ring worlds, and gateways. Unlike other megastructure achievements, repairs do count toward this total.

warnings: Habitats, ring worlds, and gateways are excluded. The count includes Dyson Spheres, Science Nexi, Sentry Arrays, and any MegaCorp/Machine Age megastructures.
curation.planner_notes: This is a late-game goal that typically requires multiple megastructure builds across the game. Prioritize cheaper megastructures (Science Nexus, Sentry Array) to reach 4 faster.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count completed megastructures or track per-type exclusions.

source.name: Machine Supremacy
source.description: Win the game as a machine uprising.
source.requirement: Achieve game victory after changing empires during an AI rebellion
source.hint: Trigger the AI-Related Incidents Situation.

conditions.notes: Requires triggering an AI rebellion, switching to the uprising, and achieving victory.

warnings: The AI rebellion event chain (AI-Related Incidents Situation) may not trigger in every run.
Synthetic ascension or owning AI servitude techs raise the chance.
curation.planner_notes: Play as a materialist empire with full AI rights research; the rebellion triggers only under specific conditions.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect machine uprising events or victory flags.

source.name: Outside Context
source.description: Invade pre-FTL Earth while it is in the midst of a world war.
source.requirement: Invade Earth, found in the Sol system, while it is in the Machine Age. Earth must spawn already in the Machine Age, not reach it via event or progress over time.
source.hint: Sol has a 50% chance to spawn regardless of galaxy size if no empires have it as its starting system.

conditions.notes: Requires finding Earth in the correct primitive era.
Requires invading that primitive Earth.

warnings: Highly dependent on Sol system generation and era.
curation.planner_notes: Treat as opportunistic unless galaxy setup can bias Sol spawning.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet classify primitive Sol era.

source.name: Planned Obsolescence
source.description: As a materialist empire, have at least 75% of the pops in your empire be robotic in an empire with at least 20,000 pops.
source.requirement: Have total Pops > 19,999 and 75% of them as robots
source.hint: Best done by pursuing Synthetic ascension path.

conditions.notes: Requires Materialist or Fanatic Materialist ethic.
Must have at least 20,000 total pops.
At least 75% of pops must be robotic.

warnings: Requires very high population and robotic conversion. Synthetic ascension strongly recommended.
curation.planner_notes: Synthetic ascension path is the most reliable way to convert a large population to robotic.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count total pops or robot pop ratio.

source.name: Put a Ring On It
source.description: Have a ring world section as your capital.
source.requirement: Designate a Ring World segment as your empire's capital
source.hint: Either go the route of building a ringworld, repairing one or capturing one from a Fallen Empire.

conditions.notes: Requires moving the capital to a Ring World segment. The capital designation decision on a ring world segment is what triggers the achievement.

warnings: Relocating the capital costs influence and requires the target planet to be a fully colonised ring world segment.
curation.planner_notes: Build a new Ring World, repair a ruined one (the Cybrex chain), or capture one from a Fallen Empire, then designate a segment as the capital via the planet decisions menu.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect the capital planet's class or capital designation status.

source.name: Then Virgil, Now Beatrice
source.description: Return a long-dead species to life.
source.requirement: Complete the In Limbo anomaly either after having researched synths, or prior to accepting an Enigmatic Observers Fallen Empire's relevant request following.
source.hint: See Limbo for event chain details.

conditions.notes: Requires finding and completing the In Limbo anomaly on a specific habitable world, then choosing to revive the species.

warnings: The In Limbo anomaly is RNG-based — it may not spawn in a given game.
curation.planner_notes: Aggressive early exploration improves the chance of finding the anomaly. The preferred path is to complete it after researching Synthetics technology.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect specific anomaly completions or the In Limbo event chain.

source.name: Tourist Trap
source.description: Own a planet with at least 10 different species on it.
source.requirement: Number of different species on a planet > 9
source.hint: Can be obtained from the start if the First Contact DLC is installed via the Broken Shackles origin.

conditions.notes: Requires at least 10 different species present on a single owned planet.

warnings: Requires migration treaties, refugees, conquest, or the Broken Shackles origin to gather enough species.
curation.planner_notes: The Broken Shackles origin (First Contact DLC) starts with multiple species. Alternatively, use migration treaties and a high-traffic habitat to collect diverse pops.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count species diversity per planet.

source.name: Unboxing
source.description: Open up a shielded world.
source.requirement: Complete the project to lower the barrier on a shielded world
source.hint: Shielded worlds appear inside the borders of the Fanatic Xenophobe Fallen Empire.

conditions.notes: Requires completing the special project to lower the shield on a shielded world.

warnings: Shielded worlds only appear within the borders of the Fanatic Xenophobe Fallen Empire.
curation.planner_notes: Wait for the Fanatic Xenophobe Fallen Empire to open borders or conquer their territory to access the shielded world.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect shielded world status or the barrier-lowering project.

source.name: Very Open Borders
source.description: As a xenophilic empire, have migration treaties with at least 10 other empires.
source.requirement: Be a Xenophile or Fanatic Xenophile empire and have at least 10 migration treaties
source.hint: Force-spawning xenophilic empires and releasing sectors as vassals will make this achievement easier to obtain.

conditions.notes: Xenophile or Fanatic Xenophile
Requires 10 active migration treaties.

warnings: Galaxy must contain enough empires willing to sign migration treaties.
curation.planner_notes: Force-spawn xenophilic empires and release sectors as vassals to increase available treaty partners. Keep relations high.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads ethics but does not yet count migration treaties.

source.name: Deus Vult
source.description: As a spiritualistic empire, own 4 holy worlds.
source.requirement: Colonize 4 worlds with the Holy World modifier
source.hint: This achievement becomes much easier if DLCs are enabled.

conditions.notes: Requires a spiritualist or fanatic spiritualist ethic to safely colonize holy worlds without immediate war.
Requires colonizing 4 planets with the Holy World modifier.

warnings: Holy Worlds are owned by the Holy Guardians or Doctrinal Enforcers Fallen Empire — colonizing them without a spiritualist ethic or before the FE is defeated triggers immediate war.
The holy world modifier applies to specific preset planets (e.g., Prophet's Retreat, Walled Garden) that are part of the FE's territory.
curation.planner_notes: Play as a spiritualist empire to avoid early FE retaliation. If playing non-spiritualist, you must first defeat or cripple the spiritualist FE before colonizing their holy worlds. Set Fallen Empires to at least 2 to ensure a spiritualist FE spawns.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads ethics but does not yet identify the Holy World modifier on planets or distinguish holy worlds from other colonizable worlds.

source.name: Does Not Compute
source.description: Shut down the Contingency plan.
source.requirement: Defeat the Contingency by destroying Nexus Zero-One
source.hint: Like all crises, lowering the Crisis Strength setting to 0.25x makes this a lot easier to achieve.

conditions.notes: The Contingency must be the endgame crisis that spawns.
Requires destroying the Contingency's final hub (Nexus Zero-One) and defeating all Sterilization Hubs.

warnings: Blocked if a different crisis appears. Force Contingency in galaxy settings if available.
curation.planner_notes: Lower crisis strength to 0.25x; focus on shield-damage weapons (Kinetic/Energy vs. Contingency shield-heavy fleets).
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet parse crisis state or final-hub destruction flags.

source.name: Queening
source.description: Successfully capture an extragalactic matriarch.
source.requirement: Capture a Prethoryn queen
source.hint: Like all crises, lowering the Crisis Strength setting to 0.25x makes this a lot easier to achieve.

conditions.notes: Requires the Prethoryn Scourge crisis.
Requires capturing the Prethoryn queen event outcome.

warnings: Blocked if a different crisis is selected or appears, unless crisis settings force Prethoryn.
curation.planner_notes: Force Prethoryn in galaxy settings if available for a targeted run.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet parse crisis state or queen capture flags.

source.name: Rift Sealed
source.description: Destroy the portals employed by the invaders from another time and space.
source.requirement: Defeat the Extradimensional Invaders by destroying the final Dimensional Portal
source.hint: Like all crises, lowering the Crisis Strength setting to 0.25x makes this a lot easier to achieve.

conditions.notes: The Extradimensional Invaders (Unbidden) must be the endgame crisis.
Requires destroying the final Dimensional Portal.

warnings: Blocked if a different crisis appears. Force Unbidden in galaxy settings if available.
curation.planner_notes: Lower crisis strength to 0.25x; Unbidden fleets are weak to Kinetic weapons.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet parse crisis state or portal destruction flags.

source.name: Voight-Kampff
source.description: Develop the means to prevent nefarious infiltration.
source.requirement: Find a way to stop the synthetic infiltration
source.hint: Complete the event chain to detect synthetic infiltrators during the Contingency crises.

conditions.notes: The Contingency must be the endgame crisis that spawns.
Requires completing the unique event chain for detecting synthetic infiltrators spawned during the Contingency.

warnings: This event chain only fires during the Contingency crisis; requires specific conditions to trigger.
curation.planner_notes: Force the Contingency crisis in galaxy settings. The detection event chain should start automatically.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet parse crisis state or contingency-specific event flags.

source.name: What Was Will Be
source.description: Close the loop, or don't.
source.requirement: Complete the Horizon Signal event chain and either outcome
source.hint: The difficulty lies in having the Horizon Signal event spawn - there is a 20% chance at the start of each game.

conditions.notes: Requires completing the Horizon Signal event chain regardless of the final outcome choice.

warnings: Horizon Signal has only a 20% chance to spawn per game; it cannot be forced.
curation.planner_notes: If the Horizon Signal triggers, follow the chain through to completion. Both the accepting and rejecting outcomes satisfy the requirement.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect Horizon Signal event flags or completion.

source.name: Tend the Garden
source.description: Become the custodian of Wenkwort Artem, designating it a resort world with a Ranger Lodge and all original blockers present.
source.requirement: Wenkwort Artem has the Wenkwort Custodian modifier, Resort World designation, Ranger Lodge building and all original blockers present.
source.hint: This achievement requires the Environmentalist civic to build a Ranger Lodge.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Plantoids DLC.
The Environmentalist civic is required to build the Ranger Lodge building.

warnings: Wenkwort Artem must spawn as a unique system — this is subject to galaxy generation RNG.
Do not clear any of the original blockers on Wenkwort Artem.
Planet must be designated as a Resort World, which prevents other development.
curation.planner_notes: Start with the Environmentalist civic. When Wenkwort Artem is discovered, complete its custodian event chain. Keep all original blockers intact. Build a Ranger Lodge. Set the planet designation to Resort World. The planet cannot be your capital.
known_limitations: The current save parser cannot detect the Wenkwort Custodian modifier, planetary blocker state, or planet designation.

source.name: Fixer Upper
source.description: As an Idyllic Bloom empire, convert the junk worlds of the Ketling Star Pack into Gaia Worlds.
source.requirement: As an Idyllic Bloom empire, own every Junk Ratlings Tomb World and terraform them into Gaia Worlds.
source.hint: The Junk Ratlings systems have an 80% chance to spawn in an Huge Galaxy.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Plantoids DLC.
Must start with the Idyllic Bloom origin.

warnings: The Ketling Star Pack (Junk Ratlings) has only an 80% spawn chance in a Huge galaxy; may not appear at all.
All Junk Ratlings tomb worlds must be owned simultaneously before terraforming.
The Idyllic Bloom origin can only terraform habitable worlds into Gaia Worlds via the Bloom Blight decision.
curation.planner_notes: Play on Huge galaxy for maximum Ketling spawn chance. Locate the Ketling Star Pack, conquer or vassalize them to claim their tomb worlds. Use the Idyllic Bloom's Bloom Blight decision to terraform each Junk Ratlings world into a Gaia World.
known_limitations: The current save parser cannot detect the Idyllic Bloom special terraforming decision or Ketling-specific world ownership.

source.name: Growing Like Weeds
source.description: As a Budding species, have 25,000 Budding pops on your Capital.
source.requirement: As a Plantoid species that does not have the Clone Army origin, have 25,000 pops with the Budding trait on your capital.
source.hint: This achievement requires you to have at least 25,000 Budding trait pops on your capital.

conditions.notes: Founding species must be Plantoid.
Founding species must have the Budding trait.
The Plantoids DLC is required for the Budding trait and this achievement.

warnings: Clone Army origin blocks this achievement (explicitly excluded by the wiki requirement).
Requires 25,000 Budding pops on a single planet (the capital). Extremely high threshold.
curation.planner_notes: Maximize pop growth on capital. Avoid Clone Army origin. The Budding trait provides passive pop assembly; combine with high habitability and growth bonuses.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect species traits or count pops by trait per planet.

source.name: Mad Genius
source.description: Recruit a scientist from the Curators.
source.requirement: Recruit a scientist from a curator enclave after having developed sufficient opinion with them to do so
source.hint: Even though the Curator Enclave appears with some other DLCs as well, this achievement requires the player to own Leviathans.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Leviathans DLC.
Requires finding a Curator enclave and recruiting a scientist from them.

warnings: Curator enclave spawn is determined at galaxy generation.
curation.planner_notes: Explore to find the Curator enclave. Pay the opinion cost and recruit a scientist. Enclave spawn rate can be influenced by galaxy size and random generation.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect enclave interactions.

source.name: Patron
source.description: Support the Artisan Troupe for 10 years.
source.requirement: Become a patron of an artisan troupe enclave and wait 10 years for the initial energy credits payment to expire
source.hint: This achievement appears to require 20 years, not 10. Renew the patronage at least once.

conditions.notes: Requires becoming patron of the Artisan Troupe.

warnings: Requires Artisan Troupe enclave availability.
curation.planner_notes: Scout enclaves early and keep enough resources for patronage.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet identify enclave patronage.

source.name: The Good Stuff
source.description: Purchase a rare resource from the Traders.
source.requirement: Purchase a strategic resource from a trader enclave
source.hint: Simply purchase any resource from one of the three trader enclaves.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Leviathans DLC.
Requires finding a Trader enclave and purchasing any strategic resource from them.

warnings: Trader enclave spawn is determined at galaxy generation.
curation.planner_notes: Explore to find a Trader enclave and buy any strategic resource. Trader enclaves are one of the three basic enclave types.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect enclave interactions.

source.name: Return to Dust
source.description: Destroy an enclave.
source.requirement: Destroy an enclave station
source.hint: What DLC the enclave comes from doesn't matter but the achievement requires the player to own Leviathans.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Leviathans DLC.
Requires destroying any enclave station (Curator, Trader, or Artisan).

warnings: Destroying an enclave is a permanent aggressive action that may have diplomatic consequences.
curation.planner_notes: Build a fleet strong enough to destroy an enclave station. Any enclave type qualifies — the achievement does not distinguish between Leviathans or other DLC enclaves, but the Leviathans DLC is required.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect enclave destruction.

source.name: Dreadnought
source.description: Restore an ancient warship.
source.requirement: Defeat the Automated Dreadnought and choose to repair it
source.hint: See the Dreadnought main article for information in how to defeat it.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Leviathans DLC.
Requires the Automated Dreadnought to spawn in the galaxy.
Must defeat and then choose to repair/restore the Dreadnought.

warnings: The Automated Dreadnought leviathan must be present in the galaxy (RNG spawn).
curation.planner_notes: Build a strong late-game fleet, locate the Dreadnought, defeat it, and pick the repair option in the event.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet identify leviathan defeat or event choice outcomes.

source.name: Hear me Roar
source.description: Hatch the egg.
source.requirement: Defeat the Ether Drake, place a mining station on Dragon Hoard and choose to incubate the dragon egg once the event fires
source.hint: The MTTH for this event is 600 months after the mining station is placed.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Leviathans DLC.
Requires the Ether Drake leviathan to spawn.
Requires defeating the Ether Drake, placing a mining station, and hatching the egg via event (MTTH 600 months).

warnings: The Ether Drake spawns on a random shielded system; not guaranteed to be in every game.
curation.planner_notes: After defeating the Ether Drake, place a mining station on the Dragon Hoard and wait for the incubation event (long MTTH).
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet identify leviathan defeat or egg-related event flags.

source.name: Infinite Creation
source.description: Birth a new universe.
source.requirement: Peacefully complete the Infinity Sphere event, resulting in Gargantua transforming into Pantagruel
source.hint: Agree to assist the Infinity Sphere and complete the 1800 day special project.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Leviathans DLC.
The Infinity Sphere (Gargantua) leviathan must spawn in the galaxy.
Must agree to assist the Infinity Sphere and peacefully complete the 1800-day special project.

warnings: The Infinity Sphere is a random leviathan spawn and is not guaranteed to appear.
The peaceful completion path requires agreeing to assist the sphere, not attacking it.
The 1800-day special project must be allowed to complete without interruption.
curation.planner_notes: When the Infinity Sphere is found, choose the option to assist it. Protect the science ship completing the 1800-day special project from interference.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect leviathan spawns or track Infinity Sphere event completion.

source.name: Stellar Performance
source.description: Take a trophy from a stellar being.
source.requirement: Defeat the Stellar Devourer and obtain the Devourer Egg Sac planetary modifier
source.hint: After you have defeated it, pick the "Retrieve the egg sac" option and wait for the parade situation to succeed.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Leviathans DLC.
Requires the Stellar Devourer leviathan to spawn.
Requires choosing to retrieve the egg sac after defeating the Stellar Devourer and completing the parade situation.

warnings: The Stellar Devourer must spawn in the galaxy; not guaranteed.
curation.planner_notes: After defeat, pick the 'Retrieve the egg sac' option and wait for the parade situation to succeed.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect leviathan-specific event outcomes.

source.name: Unravelling Enigma
source.description: Uncover the secrets of an ancient fortress.
source.requirement: Defeat and complete the event chain of the Enigmatic Fortress
source.hint: The Enigmatic Fortress article contains a guide to complete the event chain.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Leviathans DLC.
Requires the Enigmatic Fortress leviathan to spawn.
Requires completing the full Enigmatic Fortress event chain (defeat + puzzle).

warnings: The Enigmatic Fortress spawn is RNG-dependent.
curation.planner_notes: After defeating the fortress, work through the multi-stage event chain to unlock the secrets.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track Enigmatic Fortress completion flags.

source.name: Warrior of Light
source.description: Destroy a wraith.
source.requirement: Defeat the Spectral Wraith
source.hint: See the Spectral Wraith main article for information in how to defeat it.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Leviathans DLC.
Requires defeating the Spectral Wraith.

warnings: The Spectral Wraith spawns only after an empire researches Psi Jump Drives; it may not appear in every game.
curation.planner_notes: The Spectral Wraith spawns from the empire that first researches Psi Jump Drives, attacking its territory. Build up fleet power in anticipation.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect Spectral Wraith presence or defeat.

source.name: Whence It Came
source.description: Defeat a horrifying invader.
source.requirement: Defeat the Dimensional Horror
source.hint: See the Dimensional Horror main article for information in how to defeat it.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Leviathans DLC.
Requires the Dimensional Horror leviathan to spawn.
Requires defeating the Dimensional Horror.

warnings: The Dimensional Horror spawn is RNG-dependent.
curation.planner_notes: Build a strong mid-to-late-game fleet and locate the Dimensional Horror's system.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect leviathan defeat flags.

source.name: Last, Best Hope
source.description: Lead the non-aligned powers to victory against an Awakened Empire.
source.requirement: Be selected as the president of the League of Non-Aligned Empires during a War in Heaven and defeat both Awakened Empires.
source.hint: Despite what the achievement's description states, both Awakened Empires must be destroyed.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Leviathans DLC.
Requires the War in Heaven event (two Fallen Empires awaken and go to war).
Must be selected as president of the League of Non-Aligned Empires.
Both Awakened Empires must be destroyed or subjugated.

warnings: War in Heaven requires at least two Fallen Empires that awaken; not guaranteed.
The League president position depends on diplomatic weight among non-aligned powers.
curation.planner_notes: Maintain high diplomatic weight and strong fleet to be elected League president. Must defeat both Awakened Empires, not just win the war.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect War in Heaven, League formation, or Awakened Empire defeat.

source.name: Exterminatus
source.description: Use a World Cracker to destroy another Empire's capital planet.
source.requirement: Destroy another Empire's capital planet with a World Cracker Colossus
source.hint: Unlock and construct a World Cracker, create a vassal, release it and once the truce is over, declare war and destroy their capital.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Apocalypse DLC.
Requires building a Colossus.
Requires the World Cracker Colossus weapon.
Must target another empire's capital planet specifically.

warnings: Destroying a capital inflicts a heavy opinion penalty and removes the target empire's capital.
curation.planner_notes: The wiki suggests creating a vassal, releasing it, and destroying their capital after truce expires.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect Colossus construction or weapon assignment.

source.name: Clash of the Titans
source.description: Defeat a Fallen Empire's Titan fleet with a Titan of your own.
source.requirement: Defeat a Fallen Empire Titan fleet with your own Titan.
source.hint: When starting the game set the end-game year as late as possible to avoid Fallen Empires awakening.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Apocalypse DLC.
Requires building at least one Titan.
Requires a Fallen Empire fleet destroyed in battle while the player has a Titan present.

warnings: Fallen Empire Titan fleets only exist if the Fallen Empire has built Titans; must engage before they awaken.
Set end-game year as late as possible to avoid Awakened Empires.
curation.planner_notes: Declare war on a Fallen Empire before they awaken; ensure at least one Titan participates in a fleet battle where an FE Titan is destroyed.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet identify Titan participation in battles or Fallen Empire fleet composition.

source.name: No Khan Do
source.description: Kill the Great Khan in battle.
source.requirement: Destroy the Great Khan and their fleet in battle
source.hint: The Great Khan is killed in battle when their fleet is destroyed or defeated in a battle a second time.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Apocalypse DLC.
The Great Khan must appear (requires a Marauder empire to survive to mid-game).
Requires destroying the Great Khan's flagship in combat.

warnings: If the Great Khan dies of old age or is assassinated, the achievement cannot be earned in that run.
curation.planner_notes: Keep a strong fleet near Marauder territory; the Khan spawns from the strongest Marauder clan after mid-game starts.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect Great Khan state or Khan-in-battle flags.

source.name: Pandora's World
source.description: Use a Global Pacifier to shield a planet belonging to Fanatic Purifiers, Ravenous Swarms, or Determined Exterminators.
source.requirement: Use a Global Pacifier Colossus to shield a world belonging to a Fanatic Purifiers, Devouring Swarm or Determined Exterminator empire
source.hint: Other genocidal civics will not work.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Apocalypse DLC.
Requires building a Colossus.
Requires the Global Pacifier Colossus weapon.
Target must be a genocidal empire (Fanatic Purifiers, Devouring Swarm, or Determined Exterminator).

warnings: Only the three specified genocidal civics count; similar civics (e.g. Terravore) do not qualify.
curation.planner_notes: Force-spawn or keep a genocidal empire alive until Colossus tech is unlocked.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect Colossus construction or weapon types.

source.name: Stay on Target
source.description: Destroy another Empire's Colossus while it is in the process of firing on a planet.
source.requirement: Destroy an enemy Colossus while it is firing on a planet
source.hint: Despite what the achievement says, the Colossus needs only to be in orbit of a planet and destroyed.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Apocalypse DLC.
Requires destroying an enemy Colossus while it is orbiting a planet (per wiki, not strictly while firing).

warnings: Relies on the AI building and fielding a Colossus; can be missed if the target is destroyed elsewhere.
curation.planner_notes: Once an enemy Colossus is spotted near a planet, station a fleet in the system and attack before it jumps away.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track Colossus presence, destruction events, or firing state.

source.name: ...and Hope?
source.description: Unseal the path to the L-Cluster.
source.requirement: Be the first to open an L-Gate
source.hint: Using the zero empires trick makes getting this achievement a matter of time.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Distant Stars DLC.
Requires being the first empire in the galaxy to open an L-Gate.

warnings: AI empires may beat the player to the first L-Gate opening — the zero empires trick avoids this.
curation.planner_notes: The zero-empires galaxy setting guarantees the player is first. Otherwise, rush L-Gate Insight technologies and survey aggressively.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect L-Gate opening history.

source.name: It Followed Me Home
source.description: Befriend a Space Amoeba.
source.requirement: Make friends with a Space Amoeba
source.hint: See Lost Amoeba for event chain details.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Distant Stars DLC.
Requires the Lost Amoeba event chain where an orphaned space amoeba can be befriended.

warnings: The Lost Amoeba event is RNG-dependent — it may not spawn in every game.
curation.planner_notes: Survey many systems to increase the chance of triggering the Lost Amoeba anomaly. Choose the friendly option when the event fires.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect the Lost Amoeba event or amoeba companion flag.

source.name: 1999 A.D.
source.description: Slaughter the Voidspawn.
source.requirement: Defeat the Voidspawn
source.hint: See the Voidspawn main article for information in how to defeat it.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Distant Stars DLC.
Requires defeating the Voidspawn.

warnings: The Voidspawn emerges from a planetary colony via the 'Wormhole' anomaly chain; requires a specific anomaly chain to trigger.
curation.planner_notes: The Voidspawn event starts with a special anomaly found on habitable planets during surveying.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect the Voidspawn event chain or its defeat.

source.name: A Hump Like a Snow-Hill
source.description: Hunt the Tiyanki Matriarch.
source.requirement: Defeat the Tiyanki Matriarch
source.hint: See the Tiyanki Matriarch main article for information in how to defeat it.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Distant Stars DLC.
Requires the Tiyanki Matriarch leviathan to spawn.
Requires defeating the Tiyanki Matriarch.

warnings: The Tiyanki Matriarch spawn is RNG-dependent.
curation.planner_notes: Locate and defeat the Tiyanki Matriarch with a mid-game fleet.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect leviathan defeat flags.

source.name: Who Scraps the Scrapper
source.description: Destroy the Scrapper Bot.
source.requirement: Defeat the Scavenger Bot
source.hint: See the Scavenger Bot main article for information in how to defeat it.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Distant Stars DLC.
Requires the Scavenger Bot leviathan to spawn.
Requires defeating the Scavenger Bot.

warnings: The Scavenger Bot spawn is RNG-dependent.
curation.planner_notes: Locate and defeat the Scavenger Bot with a mid-to-late-game fleet.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect leviathan defeat flags.

source.name: Destroy the People of Earth!
source.description: As the Commonwealth of Man, destroy the United Nations of Earth with your ruler being a human with a symbiotic brainslug, without blowing up their final planet.
source.requirement: Destroy the United Nations of Earth as the Commonwealth of Man while your ruler has the Brain Slug Host trait and a human portrait or a humanoid Plantoid portrait.
source.hint: Gaining the Brain Slug Host trait requires finding the Abandoned Settlements anomaly, added with Distant Stars.

conditions.notes: Requires starting as the Commonwealth of Man (locked origin).
Ruler must have the Brain Slug Host leader trait, acquired via the Abandoned Settlements anomaly.
Founding species must be Humanoid (Commonwealth of Man default).

warnings: Brain Slug Host trait depends on finding the Abandoned Settlements anomaly (Distant Stars content).
UNE must survive long enough to be destroyed without World Cracker on their last planet.
curation.planner_notes: Play as the pre-made Commonwealth of Man. Explore aggressively to trigger the Abandoned Settlements anomaly. Defeat UNE conventionally once the ruler has the trait.
known_limitations: Ruler trait parsing depends on the leader being present in the `leaders` block of the save gamestate.

source.name: Black Hole Mining
source.description: Build a Matter Decompressor.
source.requirement: Construct a complete Matter Decompressor
source.hint: Repairing a destroyed Matter Decompressor does not count.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the MegaCorp expansion.
Requires building a Matter Decompressor to completion in a black hole system. Repairs do not count.

warnings: Requires a black hole system within owned territory. New construction only — repairing a ruined Matter Decompressor does not count.
curation.planner_notes: Requires Mega-Engineering and the MegaCorp DLC. Build in any owned black hole system; the Matter Decompressor provides massive mineral income.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect completed megastructures or distinguish new construction from repairs.

source.name: Franchising
source.description: As a Corporate empire, have a branch office on 5 different empires' capitals.
source.requirement: Have the Corporate government type and have branch offices on other empires' capitals > 4
source.hint: This achievement will not unlock if playing as a Criminal Heritage.

conditions.notes: Requires Megacorp authority.
This achievement requires the MegaCorp DLC.

warnings: Criminal Heritage civic blocks this achievement despite being a Megacorp.
Requires at least 5 empire capitals to establish branch offices on.
curation.planner_notes: Play as a standard Megacorp (not Criminal Heritage). Build commercial pacts and establish branch offices on 5 different capitals.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads authority but does not yet count branch offices.

source.name: Inscrutable Power
source.description: I won the Galatron and all I got was this lousy achievement.
source.requirement: Obtain The Galatron
source.hint: The Galatron is a very rare reward from the Caravaneers reliquaries.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the MegaCorp DLC.
Requires obtaining the Galatron relic from Caravaneers reliquaries.

warnings: The Galatron is a very rare reward from Caravaneers reliquaries (low single-digit percentage chance).
curation.planner_notes: Build a strong trade-based economy to afford many reliquary purchases. Save scumming is a common strategy. Activating the Galatron gives all empires a casus belli to take it.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect relic ownership, including the Galatron.

source.name: Obscure Tastes
source.description: Build a Mega-Art Installation in a nebula system.
source.requirement: Construct a complete Mega Art Installation (including the Perfection stage) in a system within a nebula
source.hint: Repairing a destroyed Mega-Art Installation does not count.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the MegaCorp expansion.
Requires building a Mega-Art Installation to the Perfection stage. Repairs do not count.
The completed Mega-Art Installation must be in a nebula system.
