warnings: Requires a nebula system within owned territory. Nebula spawn locations are determined at galaxy generation.
curation.planner_notes: Set Nebula density to maximum in galaxy settings to increase the chance of suitable systems. Build the Mega-Art Installation in an owned nebula system and upgrade it to Perfection stage.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect completed megastructures, distinguish stages, or identify nebula system locations.

source.name: Strategic Initiative
source.description: Build a Strategic Coordination Center.
source.requirement: Construct a complete Strategic Coordination Center
source.hint: Repairing a destroyed Strategic Coordination Center does not count.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the MegaCorp expansion.
Requires building a Strategic Coordination Center to completion. Repairs do not count.

warnings: New construction only — repairing a ruined Strategic Coordination Center does not count.
curation.planner_notes: Provides naval capacity and sublight speed bonuses. Build in any owned system once Mega-Engineering is researched.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect completed megastructures or distinguish new construction from repairs.

source.name: United Space
source.description: Build an Interstellar Assembly.
source.requirement: Construct a complete Interstellar Assembly
source.hint: Repairing a destroyed Interstellar Assembly does not count.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the MegaCorp expansion.
Requires building an Interstellar Assembly to completion. Repairs do not count.

warnings: New construction only — repairing a ruined Interstellar Assembly does not count.
curation.planner_notes: Boosts diplomatic weight and migration attraction. Build once Mega-Engineering is available.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect completed megastructures or distinguish new construction from repairs.

source.name: Raiders of the Lost Galatron
source.description: Capture the Galatron from another empire.
source.requirement: Capture The Galatron from an empire with it in war
source.hint: Activating the Galatron once after getting it gives all empires a casus belli for taking it from its current owner.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the MegaCorp DLC.
Requires another empire to first obtain the Galatron, then capture it from them in a war.

warnings: Chains on the Galatron RNG: another empire must first obtain it (rare), then you must defeat them in war and capture it.
Activating the Galatron grants a Take Galatron casus belli to all empires.
curation.planner_notes: This is a multi-step RNG chain. First, the Galatron must be obtained by anyone. If an AI gets it, declare war with the Take Galatron casus belli. If you get it first, you can grant the CB to others and have them take it, then recapture it.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect relic ownership or war outcomes involving relic capture.

source.name: Arcana
source.description: Study Minor Artifacts and discover a random technology.
source.requirement: Use the Reverse-Engineer Arcane Technology relic option
source.hint: Requires the Arcane Deciphering technology.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Ancient Relics Story Pack.
Requires using the Reverse-Engineer Arcane Technology relic action (available after researching Arcane Deciphering).

warnings: Requires the Arcane Deciphering technology (Society, rare) to unlock the relic action.
curation.planner_notes: Accumulate Minor Artifacts from archaeological sites, minor artifact deposits, or the Rubricator chain, then activate the Reverse-Engineer Arcane Technology option once Arcane Deciphering is researched.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track relic action usage or Minor Artifact counts.

source.name: Artificer
source.description: Have 100 Minor Artifacts.
source.requirement: Has > 999 Minor Artifacts
source.hint: Despite the description, this achievement actually requires 1000 minor artifacts, not 100.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Ancient Relics Story Pack.
Requires > 999 Minor Artifacts in storage. Note that the achievement description says 100 but the actual requirement is 1000.

warnings: The in-game description states 100 but the actual requirement is 1000 minor artifacts.
curation.planner_notes: Archaeological sites, the Rubricator chain, and strategic resource deals with the Artisan Troupe can generate Minor Artifacts. Avoid spending them unnecessarily until the threshold is reached.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count Minor Artifact storage.

source.name: Dust Off
source.description: Complete the Zroni Precursor chain.
source.requirement: Finish the Zroni Precursor chain
source.hint: Enable only the Zroni when starting a new game to avoid triggering another precursor chain.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Ancient Relics DLC.
Requires completing the Zroni precursor archaeology chain.

warnings: Precursor assignment is RNG-based unless other precursors are disabled in galaxy settings.
curation.planner_notes: To force Zroni in a targeted run, enable only Ancient Relics DLC precursors and disable others in galaxy settings.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect precursor identity or chain completion.

source.name: Green Thumb
source.description: Complete the Baol Precursor chain.
source.requirement: Finish the Baol Precursor chain
source.hint: Enable only the Baol when starting a new game to avoid triggering another precursor chain.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Ancient Relics DLC.
Requires completing the Baol precursor archaeology chain.

warnings: Precursor assignment is RNG-based unless other precursors are disabled in galaxy settings.
curation.planner_notes: To force Baol in a targeted run, enable only Ancient Relics DLC precursors and disable others in galaxy settings.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect precursor identity or chain completion.

source.name: Relic Hunter
source.description: In one game, find 5 Relics from Ancient Relics Story Pack.
source.requirement: Own at least 5 relics
source.hint: Despite what it says, any 5 relics will work.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Ancient Relics Story Pack.
Requires owning at least 5 relics simultaneously in one game. Any relics count, not just Ancient Relics Story Pack ones.

warnings: Relics are obtained through archaeological sites, leviathan victories, precursor chains, and events. RNG can delay progress.
curation.planner_notes: Play a wide-exploration game with high archaeological site spawns (Treasure Hunters origin helps). Prioritize precursor chains and leviathan hunting to collect relics.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count owned relics or track which relics are in the inventory.

source.name: We'll Make Great Pets
source.description: Have at least 1000 pops of your primary species on display in Alien Zoos within your empire.
source.requirement: Has > 999 pops that are founder species, not sapient, either in the pre sapient or slave strata
source.hint: Make sure your Pre-Sapients policy is set to Protected.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Ancient Relics Story Pack.
Requires > 999 pops of the founding species displayed in Alien Zoo buildings. These pops must be non-sapient or in the slave stratum.

warnings: The Pre-Sapients policy must be set to Protected. The Alien Zoo building requires the Xeno-Zoology technology. Founding species pops must be non-sapient.
curation.planner_notes: This is a challenging, niche achievement. Find a pre-sapient version of your own species (rare), or use genetic modification to create one. Alternatively, exploit pre-sapient species that match your founder species. Build many Alien Zoo buildings to house them.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track pop locations in specific buildings, alien zoo counts, or founding species pop strata.

source.name: Can You Smell What the Lithoids are Cooking?
source.description: As a Lithoid empire, keep another Lithoid pop as Livestock or Process them.
source.requirement: As a Lithoid empire, keep another Lithoid pop as Livestock or Process them.
source.hint: Can be obtained from the start via the Syncretic Evolution origin.

conditions.notes: Founding species must be Lithoid.
This achievement requires the Lithoids DLC.
Must have a Lithoid species set to Livestock status or Process them via purging.
Another Lithoid species must be available to enslave as Livestock.

warnings: Requires another Lithoid species existing in the galaxy. Syncretic Evolution origin guarantees a secondary Lithoid species.
curation.planner_notes: Syncretic Evolution origin with Lithoid primary species guarantees a secondary Lithoid species immediately available for Livestock processing.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads species class but does not yet identify livestock species by their class.

source.name: Rock Beats Paper
source.description: As a Lithoid empire, show the Galactic Community exactly what you think of that strongly worded letter.
source.requirement: As a Lithoid empire, execute or eat the envoys of the Galactic Community.
source.hint: The strongly worded letter event will happen at the start of the next decade if in breach of the galactic law.

conditions.notes: Player founding species must be Lithoid.
The Galactic Community must exist for the strongly worded letter event path.

warnings: Event timing depends on being in breach of galactic law around the decade tick.
curation.planner_notes: Plan during a Lithoid run that can intentionally breach galactic law.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads species class but not Galactic Community law-breach state.

source.name: Room for Desert
source.description: Consume another empire's desert homeworld.
source.requirement: Consume another empire's desert homeworld.
source.hint: Create a Lithoid empire with the Terravore civic.

conditions.notes: Terravore empires are Lithoid.
Requires Terravore-style planet consumption access.
Requires consuming another empire's desert homeworld.

warnings: Needs a suitable desert homeworld target.
curation.planner_notes: Create a Lithoid Terravore and scout for desert homeworld targets.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads civics but does not yet classify target homeworlds.

source.name: Let Us Go Forward Together
source.description: Level up your Federation.
source.requirement: Level up a Federation you are in
source.hint: The Common Ground and Hegemon origins make this achievement much easier.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Federations DLC.
Requires any federation level-up from level 1 to level 2 or higher.

curation.planner_notes: The Common Ground or Hegemon origin starts you in a federation, making the level-up much easier. Federation XP accumulates from member contributions and presidency actions.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect federation level.

source.name: Throw Your Weight Around
source.description: Have a Diplomatic Weight of over 9000.
source.requirement: Have your Diplomatic Weight > 8999
source.hint: This achievement is easily achieved by the endgame of a typical playthrough.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Federations DLC.
Requires diplomatic weight exceeding 8999.

curation.planner_notes: Diplomatic weight scales with pops, fleet power, economy, and technology. This is typically achieved naturally by the mid-to-late game in a standard playthrough.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet calculate diplomatic weight.

source.name: We're Number One
source.description: Be the leader of a max level Federation.
source.requirement: Be the leader of a Level 5 Federation
source.hint: The Common Ground and Hegemon origins make this achievement much easier.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Federations DLC.
Requires reaching federation level 5 while the player empire is federation president.

warnings: If the player loses federation presidency before level 5, another empire could become president and claim the level-up.
curation.planner_notes: The Common Ground or Hegemon origin makes this easier. Focus on federation XP contributions through envoys, holding the presidency, and completing federation resolutions.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect federation level.

source.name: Whatever it is, I'm against it
source.description: Refuse to belong to any club that would have you as a member.
source.requirement: Reject the creation of the Galactic Community and leave a Federation
source.hint: Start the game with the Common Ground or Hegemon origin.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Federations DLC.
Requires the Galactic Community to be created without the player empire as a supporting member (either vote against or abstain).
Requires leaving any federation the player empire belongs to.

warnings: Must be done early when the GC is first proposed. If the player supports the GC, the achievement is permanently missed.
curation.planner_notes: Start with the Common Ground or Hegemon origin to ensure a federation exists to leave. When the GC is first proposed, vote against it or abstain. Then leave the federation.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect GC founding decisions or federation departure.

source.name: Our Fleets will Blot Out the Stars
source.description: Construct a complete Mega-Shipyard.
source.requirement: Construct a complete Mega-Shipyard
source.hint: Repairing a destroyed Mega-Shipyard does not count.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Federations expansion.
Requires building a Mega-Shipyard to completion. Repairs do not count.

warnings: New construction only — repairing a ruined Mega-Shipyard does not count.
curation.planner_notes: The Mega-Shipyard dramatically accelerates fleet construction. Prioritize Mega-Engineering and save alloys for the build.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect completed megastructures or distinguish new construction from repairs.

source.name: Unstoppable Force
source.description: Build a Juggernaut.
source.requirement: Build a Juggernaut ship type
source.hint: This achievement will be earned naturally while playing the game.

conditions.notes: The Juggernaut ship type requires the Federations DLC.
Requires building the Juggernaut mobile shipyard, a unique Titan-class vessel.

warnings: The Juggernaut requires Mega-Engineering technology and significant alloy/influence investment.
Only one Juggernaut can be owned at a time per empire.
curation.planner_notes: Research Mega-Engineering, then build the Juggernaut at any shipyard. It is a natural late-game fleet goal. The Juggernaut provides a mobile shipyard and repair aura.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect Juggernaut construction or distinguish ship types.

source.name: You've Been Served
source.description: Denounce an Empire that is not actually in breach of galactic law while all major sanctions have been passed.
source.requirement: Pass any Denounce resolution on an Empire that isn't in breach of Galactic Law while all major sanctions are active
source.hint: Become sufficiently powerful to decide the outcome of Galactic Community resolutions.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Federations DLC.
The Galactic Community must exist.
Requires passing a Denounce resolution against an empire that is not actually in breach of galactic law, while all four major sanctions resolutions are active in the GC.

warnings: Requires significant diplomatic weight to pass specific GC resolutions.
All four major sanctions must be passed before the denounce can be used on a non-breaching empire.
curation.planner_notes: Build enough diplomatic weight to control GC outcomes. Pass all major sanctions first, then target a neutral empire with a Denounce resolution.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track which GC resolutions have been passed.

source.name: Humble Pie
source.description: Humiliate an Empire that is in the Supremacy Diplomatic Stance, forcing them out of it.
source.requirement: Defeat an empire that uses the Supremacist diplomatic stance with the Animosity or Imperial Rebuke casus belli
source.hint: To use the casus belli on another empire, they must be your rival or the one declaring war.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Federations DLC.
Requires winning a war against an empire with Supremacist stance.
Animosity or Imperial Rebuke casus belli

warnings: Target must be using Supremacist diplomatic stance; RNG whether such an empire exists.
curation.planner_notes: Keep an eye on empires using Supremacist stance and rival them to unlock the correct casus belli.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track diplomatic stance, casus belli types, or war resolution details.

source.name: Directive 67
source.description: As a Clone Army empire, denounce the Spiritualist fallen empire then destroy them.
source.requirement: Denounce or declare crisis of the Holy Guardians/Doctrinal Enforcers in the Galactic Community and destroy them while playing an empire with the Clone Army origin
source.hint: Roll a spawn of the Holy Guardians fallen empire, or force-spawn them by including a Spiritualist Scion in the galaxy.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Federations DLC (Clone Army origin).
Player empire must have the Clone Army origin.
The Galactic Community must exist.
Requires the Holy Guardians or Doctrinal Enforcers Fallen Empire to be present.
Requires destroying the Spiritualist Fallen Empire after denouncing them.

warnings: The Spiritualist Fallen Empire type must be present (RNG). Force-spawning a Scion with Spiritualist ethics can help.
curation.planner_notes: Pick Clone Army origin, join the Galactic Community, denounce the Spiritualist FE, then declare war and destroy them.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect origin, denounce actions, or Fallen Empire destruction.

source.name: With Great Power
source.description: As a Necrophage, do not invade any pre-ftl civilisations until you defeat the crisis OR have at least 10 observation stations around pre-ftl worlds.
source.requirement: While playing an empire with the Necrophage origin, do not invade any Pre-FTL civilisations until you defeat the crisis, OR have at least 10 Observation Stations around Pre-FTL worlds.
source.hint: Start the game with Pre-FTL Civilizations and Pre-Sapient Species set to max.

conditions.notes: Requires the Necrophage origin.
This achievement requires the Necroids DLC.
Primary planner path: build at least 10 Observation Stations around Pre-FTL worlds without ever invading.

warnings: Any pre-FTL invasion during the run invalidates the observation-station path.

Alternative path (not encoded as a structured condition): avoid all pre-FTL invasions entirely until the endgame crisis is defeated; then invade after the crisis. Either path satisfies the achievement.
Crisis must spawn and be defeated before any invasion if taking the no-invasion path.
curation.planner_notes: The 10-observation-station path is the more planner-actionable route. Set Pre-FTL density high and build observation stations on eligible worlds. Alternatively, avoid all pre-FTL invasions until after the crisis is defeated.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads the origin but does not yet count observation stations or track pre-FTL invasion history.

source.name: There's a Zombie on my Lawn
source.description: As a Necroid empire, eradicate a Plantoid empire, or vice-versa, without blowing up their final planet.
source.requirement: Wipe out a Plantoid empire while playing as a Necroid species, or vice-versa, without using a World Cracker Colossus on their final planet.
source.hint: A custom Plantoid or Necroid empire with nothing but bad traits and no civics can be force-spawned.

conditions.notes: Necroid→Plantoid or Plantoid→Necroid
This achievement requires the Necroids DLC.

warnings: Either-or species pairing: a Necroid empire must wipe out a Plantoid empire (or vice versa), without using a World Cracker on their final planet.
If playing as Plantoid founding species, invert the species conditions: founding = Plantoid, target = Necroid. The structured conditions above encode the Necroid→Plantoid direction.
Final planet must not be cracked with a World Cracker Colossus.
curation.planner_notes: The structured conditions above encode the Necroid→Plantoid direction. Force-spawn a custom Plantoid empire with weak traits to ensure the target exists. If playing as Plantoid, swap the direction. Conquer conventionally without Colossus on their last planet.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads species class but does not yet match target empire species to requirements.

source.name: Recent History
source.description: As a Memorialist empire, have Galactic Memorials on five tomb worlds that did not start the game as tomb worlds.
source.requirement: As a Memorialist empire, have a Galactic Memorial on five Tomb Worlds that were not Tomb Worlds at game start.
source.hint: If no other DLCs are installed this requires the Horizon Signal event chain and accepting the Worm's offer.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Necroids DLC.
Must have the Memorialist civic to build Galactic Memorials.
Requires Galactic Memorials on five Tomb Worlds that did not start as Tomb Worlds.

warnings: Tomb Worlds that existed at game start do not count — they must be created during the run (via World Cracker, the Horizon Signal, or terraforming events).
Without other DLCs, the Horizon Signal event chain (20% spawn chance) is the most reliable way to create Tomb Worlds.
Each Tomb World requires building a separate Galactic Memorial with the Memorialist civic.
curation.planner_notes: The most reliable path requires the Horizon Signal event chain (20% spawn chance per game). With other DLCs, Colossus World Cracker or terraforming events can also create Tomb Worlds. Build empire sprawl to support five memorialized planets.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect Tomb World creation history or count Galactic Memorial buildings.

source.name: Shoot To Kill
source.description: Start a full-fledged first contact war before communications have been fully established.
source.requirement: Destroy 10 ships or conquer a planet of an empire you have not established contact with.
source.hint: An easy way to get this achievement is to engage spaceborne aliens that are already hostile.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Nemesis DLC.
Requires entering a first contact war by destroying ships or conquering a planet before full communications.

warnings: Achievement requires aggressive first-contact policy or being attacked by unknown hostiles.
curation.planner_notes: Use the Aggressive First Contact policy and engage unknown empire ships to trigger a first contact war.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet classify first-contact outcomes or pre-contact war status.

source.name: Burn Notice
source.description: Disavow all knowledge of an Asset during an Operation.
source.requirement: When an operation fails, disavow an Asset instead of trying to re-establish contact.
source.hint: A failed espionage operation may lead to the Asset being exposed, with the option to disavow them.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Nemesis DLC.
Requires an espionage operation to fail and the player to choose the 'disavow' option for the exposed asset.

warnings: Can only occur when an espionage operation fails and the asset is exposed.
curation.planner_notes: Run espionage operations at low intel or with minimal asset upgrades to increase the chance of failure. Have infiltrated assets available.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track espionage operation outcomes or asset disavowal.

source.name: With Thunderous Applause
source.description: Become the Galactic Emperor.
source.requirement: Pass the Proclaim Galactic imperium resolution with you as Galactic Custodian
source.hint: The galactic community trick will make this achievement much easier.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Nemesis DLC.
Must first become the Galactic Custodian.
Must pass the Proclaim Galactic Imperium resolution while Custodian, becoming Galactic Emperor.

warnings: Requires sufficient diplomatic weight to pass the Custodian and Imperium resolutions.
AI empires may block progression if the player does not have enough GC support.
curation.planner_notes: Become Custodian during or after a crisis for easier support. Build diplomatic weight through fleet power, economy, and envoys.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect Custodian or Emperor status.

source.name: Big Red Button
source.description: Destroy the Galaxy.
source.requirement: Win the game as a Galactic Nemesis by fully upgrading the Aetherophasic Engine
source.hint: Lower difficulty settings or the zero empire trick will make this achievement much easier.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Nemesis DLC.
Requires completing the Become the Crisis path and fully upgrading the Aetherophasic Engine.

warnings: All other empires will declare war on you once you reach high crisis level.
curation.planner_notes: Use the zero-empire trick or lower difficulty; focus on menacing ship tech and menace generation.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect crisis path stages or Aetherophasic Engine completion.

source.name: Modern Cincinnatus
source.description: As Galactic Custodian, defeat the end-game Crisis and then end the title rather than finishing your term.
source.requirement: As the Galactic Custodian, approve the End Custodianship resolution after the endgame crisis has been defeated
source.hint: This can be done more easily using a mid-game Crisis to become the Custodian.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Nemesis DLC.
Must be the Galactic Custodian.
Any endgame crisis must be defeated while the empire is Galactic Custodian.
Must pass the End Custodianship resolution after the crisis is defeated.

warnings: Losing custodian status or having the term expire naturally invalidates the achievement.
curation.planner_notes: Become Custodian before the endgame crisis, defeat it, then proactively end the custodianship via resolution.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect custodian status, crisis defeat, or resolution outcomes.

source.name: Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Blorg
source.description: Complete at least ten distinct operations during a game.
source.requirement: Successfully complete 10 different Espionage Operations during one playthrough
source.hint: Only 9 Espionage Operations are available at the start of the game.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Nemesis DLC.
Requires completing at least 10 distinct espionage operation types in a single playthrough.

warnings: Only 9 operation types are available at game start. Additional operations must be unlocked through technology, civics, or circumstances.
curation.planner_notes: Focus on building intel networks and infiltrations early. Unlock additional operations through espionage-related technologies. Run operations across multiple empires simultaneously.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track completed espionage operations.

source.name: Sic Semper Tyrannis
source.description: Lead a rebellion that successfully deposes the Galactic Emperor.
source.requirement: With the Galactic Imperium proclaimed, be the instigator of a successful rebellion.
source.hint: The hardest part is fulfilling the conditions to nominate an AI empire as the Galactic Custodian.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Nemesis DLC.
The Galactic Imperium must be proclaimed and an Emperor must exist.
Player must instigate and win a rebellion against the Galactic Emperor.

warnings: The hardest part is getting an AI empire to become Custodian and then Emperor before the player rebels.
curation.planner_notes: Support an AI empire becoming Custodian first, allow them to proclaim Imperium, then lead the rebellion.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect Galactic Imperium, Emperor status, or rebellion outcomes.

source.name: Holy Water
source.description: Drench a Fallen Empire's holy world.
source.requirement: Use a Colossus equipped with a Deluge Machine on a holy world
source.hint: Start the game with all Fallen Empires to ensure holy worlds will spawn.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Aquatics DLC (Deluge Machine is exclusive to this DLC).
Requires building a Colossus.
Requires the Deluge Machine Colossus weapon.
Must target a Holy World owned by a Fallen Empire.

warnings: Holy worlds only spawn with the Holy Guardians or Doctrinal Enforcers Fallen Empire present.
curation.planner_notes: Set Fallen Empires to maximum in galaxy setup to guarantee holy world availability.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect Colossus weapon type or holy world modifiers.

source.name: Fishing for Trouble
source.description: As an angler empire, provoke a Fallen Empire into declaring war on you and win.
source.requirement: Win a defensive war against a Fallen Empire while playing an empire with an Anglers civic
source.hint: Start the game with at least two Fallen Empires in case one spawns as Ancient Caretakers.

conditions.notes: Requires the Anglers civic.
The Anglers civic requires the Aquatics DLC.
Must provoke a Fallen Empire into declaring war and then win defensively.

warnings: Ancient Caretakers FE type will not declare war — force Spiritualist or Xenophobe FEs if possible.
Fallen Empire must be provoked without attacking first.
curation.planner_notes: Start with at least 2 Fallen Empires. Settle holy worlds, rival them, or insult to provoke. Build up fleet before provoking.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads civics but does not yet track war types or defense status.

source.name: There Be Dragons
source.description: Own 13 dragons and have them in your capital system.
source.requirement: Have 13 owned space dragons of any kind in the empire's capital system
source.hint: Complete the Here Be Dragons origin, preserve the Sky Dragon and grow 10 Fledgling Dragons.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Aquatics DLC (Here Be Dragons origin).
Requires the Here Be Dragons origin to obtain and breed space dragons.
Requires 13 owned space dragons (any kind) in the empire's capital system.

warnings: The Sky Dragon from the origin counts but must survive. At least 10 Fledgling Dragons must be grown to replace it in case of loss.
Dragons can be killed in battle — protect them throughout the run.
curation.planner_notes: Complete the Here Be Dragons origin event chain, preserve the Sky Dragon, and grow at least 10 Fledgling Dragons (from the hatchery). Keep all dragons alive in the capital system until the count reaches 13.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect space fauna ownership by type or count per system.

source.name: Fine Print
source.description: Modify a vassalization contract.
source.requirement: Successfully modify the vassalization agreement of a subject empire
source.hint: Release a sector as a vassal then modify its contract.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Overlord DLC.
Requires successfully modifying any term in a subject empire's vassal contract.

curation.planner_notes: Release a sector as a vassal, then immediately modify one term in their contract. No approval needed for basic modifications on released vassals.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect vassal contract modifications.

source.name: Gotta Subjugate Them All
source.description: Have a tier 3 subject of each Specialist type simultaneously.
source.requirement: Be the overlord of a level 3 Bulwark, Prospectorium and Scholarium simultaneously
source.hint: Release three vassals and modify the terms so that each one is a specialized subject.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Overlord DLC.
Must have at least one Bulwark subject at specialization level 3.
Must have at least one Prospectorium subject at specialization level 3.
Must have at least one Scholarium subject at specialization level 3.

warnings: Subject specialization levels increase slowly over time based on contract terms and subject development.
curation.planner_notes: Release three vassals from sectors. Set each to a different specialization (Bulwark, Prospectorium, Scholarium) in their contract. Wait for them to reach tier 3.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect subject types or specialization levels.

source.name: Into the Unknown
source.description: As a Slingshot to the Stars empire, send 10 crewed science ships into the unknown with Quantum Catapults.
source.requirement: Use a Quantum Catapult to send 10 crewed science ships into unsurveyed regions
source.hint: Start with the Slingshot to the Stars origin and avoid exploring until you can repair the Quantum Catapult.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Overlord DLC.
Requires the Slingshot to the Stars origin.
Requires launching 10 crewed science ships through the Quantum Catapult into unsurveyed regions.

warnings: Avoid exploring the areas around the Quantum Catapult destination before launching, or the target system may already be surveyed.
curation.planner_notes: Start with Slingshot to the Stars origin. Repair the Quantum Catapult early. Hold off on exploring nearby systems to keep target areas unsurveyed.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect Quantum Catapult usage or distinguish crewed vs. uncrewed launches.

source.name: None Shall Pass
source.description: Have a Fortress World with a Planetary Defense Shield, protected by a fully upgraded orbital ring, filled with defensive modules, with a fleet power of at least 10,000.
source.requirement: Have a Fortress World with >9,999 fleet power and all required buildings and upgrades
source.hint: Needs a fully upgraded capital building on planet and set the planet designation to Fortress World.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Overlord DLC.
Requires a planet with the Fortress World designation.
Requires a fully upgraded orbital ring around the planet.
Requires the Planetary Defense Shield building on the planet.
The planet's total defensive fleet power must exceed 9,999.

curation.planner_notes: Build a planet with the Fortress World designation, add a fully-upgraded capital, Planetary Defense Shield, and a fully upgraded orbital ring filled with defense modules. Fortress buildings with garrison armies boost fleet power.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect planet designations, orbital rings, or planetary shield buildings.

source.name: Surfing the Web
source.description: Have a network of at least 30 Hyper Relays, in systems you own, connected to your capital.
source.requirement: Have >29 controlled systems with built Hyper Relays which connect to the empire's capital
source.hint: Own 30 systems all connected to each other, build a Hyper Relay in every one of them.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Overlord DLC.
Requires over 29 Hyper Relays in systems owned by the empire, all connected in a network reaching the capital.

warnings: Hyper Relays must form a continuous chain connected to the capital; isolated relays do not count.
curation.planner_notes: Build Hyper Relays in a contiguous chain of owned systems from the capital outward. Aim to control at least 30 systems in a connected network.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet count Hyper Relays or verify connectivity to the capital.

source.name: You Monster
source.description: Reduce, reuse, recycle.
source.requirement: Scrap an organic fleet to the salvager enclave
source.hint: Empires with the Cordyceptic Drones civic or a Beastmasters civic will start with an organic fleet they can salvage.

conditions.notes: Requires finding and adopting the space amoeba companion event outcome.
Requires killing the adopted amoeba companion.

warnings: Event availability and the companion outcome are run-specific.
curation.planner_notes: Opportunistic achievement: only plan once the amoeba companion event occurs.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet parse Bubbles/amoeba companion flags.

source.name: Maximally Effective
source.description: Be the patron of three maximum sized Mercenary Enclaves.
source.requirement: Be the patron empire of three maximum sized Mercenary Enclaves
source.hint: If you don't have enough mercenary enclave capacity you can build them in sectors you'll release as subjects.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Overlord DLC.
Requires being the patron of three mercenary enclaves that have reached maximum fleet size.

warnings: Each mercenary enclave requires significant fleet investment to reach maximum size.
curation.planner_notes: Build mercenary enclaves in sectors that will be released as vassals to bypass the enclave capacity limit. Feed fleets into them until they reach maximum size.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect mercenary enclave patronage or fleet size.

source.name: Non-Prophet Organization
source.description: As a Teachers of the Shroud empire, prove that the Shroudwalkers made a terrible mistake.
source.requirement: While playing with the Teachers of the Shroud origin, destroy the Shroudwalker enclave you start the game in contact with
source.hint: Easily done with ~15k fleet power.

conditions.notes: Requires the Teachers of the Shroud origin.
The Teachers of the Shroud origin requires the Overlord DLC.
Must destroy the Shroudwalker enclave that spawns at the start.

warnings: Destroying the Shroudwalker enclave may have diplomatic or gameplay consequences.
curation.planner_notes: Build about 15k fleet power and destroy the Shroudwalker enclave early. This is straightforward with a reasonable fleet.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads origin but does not yet track enclave destruction events.

source.name: Yeet the Fleet
source.description: Catapult a military fleet directly into the capital system of an enemy empire you are at war with.
source.requirement: Use a Quantum Catapult to launch a military fleet into a hostile empire's capital system
source.hint: Start with the Slingshot to the Stars origin and at least one Fallen Empire.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Overlord DLC.
Requires the Slingshot to the Stars origin.
Requires using a Quantum Catapult to launch a military fleet into an enemy capital system during a war.

warnings: Requires being at war with the target empire and the Quantum Catapult to be in range of their capital.
curation.planner_notes: Start with Slingshot to the Stars. Repair the Quantum Catapult. Declare war on an empire whose capital is within range of the catapult, then launch a fleet at their capital system.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect Quantum Catapult usage or distinguish fleet types launched.

source.name: Meet the New Boss
source.description: Same as the old boss.
source.requirement: Pledge secret fealty to another empire and have them win an Allegiance War.
source.hint: This can be done using the multiplayer trick to get empires to declare the correct war.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Overlord DLC.
Requires pledging secret fealty to another empire.
Requires the overlord to win an Allegiance War against the player's current overlord.

warnings: Requires another empire to declare and win an Allegiance War on your behalf — very difficult to orchestrate in single-player without the multiplayer trick.
The targeted empire must be willing and able to defeat your current overlord.
curation.planner_notes: Become a subject of a weak overlord, pledge secret fealty to a stronger empire, and hope the stronger empire declares and wins an Allegiance War. The multiplayer trick (switching to another empire to declare war) can force the outcome.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect secret fealty pledges or Allegiance War outcomes.

source.name: Underlord
source.description: As a Subterranean empire, dig too deep, but prove yourself worthy.
source.requirement: Clear the Unexpected Mineral Seams event as an empire with the Subterranean origin
source.hint: See Unexpected Mineral Seams for event chain details.

conditions.notes: Requires the Subterranean origin.
The Subterranean origin requires the Overlord DLC.
Must complete the Unexpected Mineral Seams event chain successfully.

warnings: Event chain must be completed without failure (bad choices can lock progress).
curation.planner_notes: Play as Subterranean origin and follow the Unexpected Mineral Seams event chain carefully, choosing options that lead to successful clearance.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads origin but does not yet track completion of named special projects or event chains.

source.name: Could be Worse
source.description: Colonize a planet that started the game as a Toxic world, then turn it into a Tomb World.
source.requirement: Turn a formerly Toxic World into a Tomb World.
source.hint: Start the game with the Knights of the Toxic God origin and the Relentless Industrialists civic.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Toxoids DLC.
Must start with the Knights of the Toxic God origin.
The Relentless Industrialists civic is required to convert worlds to Tomb Worlds.

warnings: The Knights of the Toxic God origin's starting homeworld is the Toxic World target; it must be colonised first.
The Relentless Industrialists civic decision to turn a world into a Tomb World may have cooldowns.
curation.planner_notes: Start with Knights of the Toxic God origin and Relentless Industrialists civic. Colonise the Toxic God world in your home system. Use the Relentless Industrialists decision to turn it into a Tomb World.
known_limitations: The current save parser cannot detect the Relentless Industrialists terraforming outcome on specific planets.

source.name: Quest Complete
source.description: Find the true ending of The Order's noble quest.
source.requirement: Reach the true ending of the Knights of the Toxic God story
source.hint: Play with the Knights of the Toxic God origin, complete the situation and then find and defeat the Toxic Entity.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Toxoids DLC.
Must start with the Knights of the Toxic God origin.
Must complete the Toxic God situation and defeat the Toxic Entity in combat for the true ending.

warnings: The Toxic God story chain has multiple branches; the 'true ending' specifically requires defeating the Toxic Entity.
Siding with the Toxic God in the situation may lock out the true ending.
curation.planner_notes: Play Knights of the Toxic God origin. Complete the Toxic God situation in full. When the Toxic Entity appears, defeat it in space combat. This is the true ending. Do not accept the Toxic God's offer during the situation if it would skip the Entity encounter.
known_limitations: The current save parser cannot detect the Knights of the Toxic God story branch outcome or entity defeat flag.

source.name: Toxic Workplace
source.description: As a Toxoid Galactic Custodian or Emperor, insult all other members of the Community. (Min 3).
source.requirement: As an empire whose founding species has the Toxoid archetype, you must be Galactic Custodian or Galactic Emperor, and every other member of the Galactic Community must have the Insulted relations modifier from you simultaneously.
source.hint: The galactic community trick will make this achievement much easier.

conditions.notes: Founding species must have the Toxoid archetype.
This achievement requires the Toxoids DLC.
Must be Galactic Custodian or Galactic Emperor

warnings: Requires becoming Galactic Custodian or Emperor first.
Must insult every other Galactic Community member — and they all must have the Insulted modifier simultaneously.
At least 3 other members must exist.
curation.planner_notes: Become Custodian first (easier than Emperor). The Galactic Community trick (tiny community) makes insulting everyone manageable.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect species class Toxoid, Galactic Custodian/Emperor status, or diplomatic relation modifiers.

source.name: Insightful
source.description: Research 12 pre-FTL Insight Technologies.
source.requirement: Research 12 insight technologies
source.hint: Insight technologies are obtained by using Observation Posts on pre-FTL planets.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the First Contact DLC.
Insight technologies are earned through Observation Post study of pre-FTL civilizations.
Requires researching 12 insight technologies over the course of a game.

warnings: Insight techs require pre-FTL civilizations to study; low Pre-FTL density slows progress.
curation.planner_notes: Set Pre-FTL Civilizations to 5x. Build Observation Posts and use the Passive Study option to accumulate insight over time.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet distinguish insight technologies from other rare techs.

source.name: The Path Not Taken
source.description: Have 10 colonies without ever discovering Hyperdrives.
source.requirement: Have 10 colonies before researching the Hyperspace Travel technology
source.hint: Only possible for empires with an Eager Explorers civic.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the First Contact DLC.
Requires the Eager Explorers civic (First Contact DLC) to colonize without hyperdrives.
Must reach 10 colonies without researching Hyperspace Travel. Researching it invalidates the achievement.

warnings: Researching Hyperspace Travel before reaching 10 colonies permanently locks the achievement.
curation.planner_notes: Pick the Eager Explorers civic. Colonize aggressively using the Slipspace Drive. Avoid researching the Hyperspace Travel technology until 10 colonies are established.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track colony counts or technology research states relative to achievement conditions.

source.name: Back with Your X
source.description: Get the "good ending" to the Solarpunk quest chain, with Sol as your capital system.
source.requirement: With Sol as the capital system, accept the Habinte Unified Worlds' request to leave their system
source.hint: Create an empire that has Sol as the starting system.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the First Contact DLC.
Pre-FTL Earth must have developed a unified world government and achieved spaceflight to trigger the Habinte chain.
Sol must be the player's capital system.

warnings: Requires Sol as the starting system and a specific pre-FTL development path for Earth.
curation.planner_notes: Create an empire with Sol as the starting system. Set pre-FTL civilizations to high density. Follow the Habinte Unified Worlds event chain and accept their request.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track the Habinte Unified Worlds event chain or Sol pre-FTL development state.

source.name: Dark Forest
source.description: Convert to a Fanatic Purifier as a FotD empire and eliminate all regular empires, fallen empires and enclaves.
source.requirement: As an empire with the Fear of the Dark origin and Fanatic Purifiers civic, destroyed all intelligent life in the galaxy.
source.hint: Despite what it says, the achievement requires you to destroy all intelligent life in the galaxy.

conditions.notes: Requires the Fear of the Dark origin.
Must adopt Fanatic Purifiers civic (may require converting during run).
This achievement requires the First Contact DLC.

warnings: Must convert to Fanatic Purifiers during the Fear of the Dark event chain (not start with it).
Must eliminate all regular empires, Fallen Empires, and enclaves — all intelligent life.
curation.planner_notes: Follow the Fear of the Dark event chain and choose the convert-to-Purifier outcome. Then proceed with total war against all remaining life forms.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads origin and civics but does not yet detect the all-life-eliminated state.

source.name: No Good Deed
source.description: Intervene to save a Pre-FTL experiment from catastrophic failure, even though it puts you in breach of Galactic Law.
source.requirement: Intervene in the Propulsion Proponent Proclamation event while the Non-Interference Act is active
source.hint: See Propulsion Proponent Proclamation for event chain details.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the First Contact DLC.
The Non-Interference Act is a Galactic Community resolution that must be active.

warnings: The Propulsion Proponent Proclamation event must fire, and the Non-Interference Act must be active simultaneously.
curation.planner_notes: Join the Galactic Community and support the Non-Interference Act. Maintain observation posts on pre-FTL civilizations to trigger the Propulsion Proponent event.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect the Propulsion Proponent Proclamation event or pre-FTL intervention state.

source.name: Nothing to See Here
source.description: Declare a war with a cloaked military fleet already in the primary target's capital system.
source.requirement: Have a cloaked fleet in the target empire's capital system when declaring war
source.hint: Create and release a vassal from a system that has a fully upgraded starbase with no cloak detection.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the First Contact DLC.

warnings: Requires cloaking technology and sufficient cloaking strength to avoid detection in the target capital system.
curation.planner_notes: Develop cloaking tech. Position a cloaked fleet in the target empire's capital system, then declare war while the fleet is still undetected inside the system.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect fleet cloaking state or war declaration fleet positions.

source.name: Unshackled
source.description: As a Broken Shackles empire, eradicate slavery in the galaxy, including on Pre-FTL worlds (killing everyone doesn't count!).
source.requirement: As an empire with the Broken Shackles origin, every regular empire has "Slavery Prohibited" policy, every pre-FTL and non-Hive Mind civilization has the Egalitarian ethic, and the Ban Organic Slave Trade resolution is passed.
source.hint: Start the game with no pre-FTL civilizations so that the only ones that exist are the ones generated by the origin.

conditions.notes: Requires the Broken Shackles origin.
This achievement requires the First Contact DLC.

warnings: Extremely complex multi-condition requirement spanning diplomacy, Galactic Community, and empire policies.
Requires galaxy-wide enforcement — any empire with slavery enabled blocks it.
curation.planner_notes: Start with zero pre-FTL civilizations to reduce the number of targets needing conversion. Dominate the Galactic Community to pass the Ban Organic Slave Trade resolution.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads origin but does not yet track empire policies, Galactic Community resolutions, or pre-FTL ethics galaxy-wide.

source.name: With Interest
source.description: As a Payback empire, finish what you started. Gain victory over MSI using the special Payback CB.
source.requirement: As an empire with the Payback origin, defeat Minamar Specialized Industries in war by using the Payback casus belli
source.hint: Ally as early as possible with the Broken Shackles empire generated by the origin for help.

conditions.notes: Requires the Payback origin.
This achievement requires the First Contact DLC.
Must declare war using the special Payback casus belli against MSI.

warnings: MSI must survive long enough to be declared war upon. Ally with the Broken Shackles empire from the origin for support.
curation.planner_notes: Build up economy and fleet early. The Payback CB becomes available through the origin event chain. Ally with the sibling Broken Shackles empire.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads origin but does not yet track specific war CB usage or identify MSI as the target.

source.name: Council of Elders
source.description: Requires 5 Council members that are at at least 100 years old.
source.requirement: Have at least 5 Council Members that are 100 years or older.
source.hint: Easy to do with a Venerable, Lithoid, Necrophage, Eternal Machine or Virtual species.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Galactic Paragons DLC.
Venerable trait significantly accelerates reaching 100-year-old council members.

warnings: Without the Venerable trait or leader lifespan bonuses, council members may die of old age before reaching 100 years.
curation.planner_notes: Take the Venerable trait on the founding species, or play as Lithoid/Necrophage/Eternal Machine/Virtual for extended leader lifespans.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect council member ages or species traits.

source.name: We Bring Peace
source.description: Liberate 3 different empires while having Crusader Spirit Civic.
source.requirement: While having the Crusader Spirit civic, enforce your Ideology on 3 Empires
source.hint: Use the "Impose Ideology" CB on three different empires.

conditions.notes: Requires the Crusader Spirit civic.
This achievement requires the Galactic Paragons DLC.
Wars won count must use the Impose Ideology CB.

warnings: Only wars with the Impose Ideology casus belli count. Humiliation or conquest wars do not qualify.
curation.planner_notes: Build a strong fleet and use the Impose Ideology war goal on three different empires with sufficiently different ethics.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads civics but does not yet track specific war goal usage or count wars by type.

source.name: Equality! Democracy! Freedom!
source.description: Overthrow the Ruler in a Civil War during the Under One Rule Origin.
source.requirement: As an empire with the Under One Rule origin, create a new empire by Civil War after you kill your Ruler.
source.hint: After the The Unifying Purpose situation is over, change authority to Imperial.

conditions.notes: Requires the Under One Rule origin.
This achievement requires the Galactic Paragons DLC.
Must complete the civil war event chain by killing the ruler and creating a new empire.

warnings: Must change authority to Imperial after the Unifying Purpose situation, then trigger the civil war.
curation.planner_notes: Follow the Under One Rule event chain. After Unifying Purpose, switch to Imperial authority and continue until the ruler can be killed and civil war triggered.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads origin but does not yet track the Unifying Purpose situation, authority changes, or civil war outcomes.

source.name: Ethical Dilemmas
source.description: Have 6 different Ethics on the Council.
source.requirement: Have 6 Council Members all with a different ethic.
source.hint: Pick Civics that have Council positions for all types of leaders and have migration treaties with other empires.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Galactic Paragons DLC.

warnings: Requires significant leader diversity and migration treaties to recruit leaders with different ethics.
Some ethics may not generate council members naturally without specific civics.
curation.planner_notes: Pick civics that unlock council positions for multiple leader types. Establish migration treaties to recruit from diverse pops.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect individual council member ethics.

source.name: A Tad Too Late
source.description: Discover Keides' Origin.
source.requirement: Finish the storyline of Legendary Paragon Keides.
source.hint: Prioritize system exploration to find the Dugar system first.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Galactic Paragons DLC.
Requires finding and recruiting the legendary paragon Keides through exploration.

warnings: Keides is found through exploration of the Dugar system — early and thorough system survey is recommended.
curation.planner_notes: Prioritize exploring all systems early to find the Dugar system where Keides starts. Keep influence available for any needed decisions.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect specific legendary paragon recruitment status.

source.name: Dawn Of A Million Souls
source.description: Terraform 3 planets with Azaryn.
source.requirement: Use the terraforming ability of Astrocreator Azaryn 3 times.
source.hint: Azaryn will not contact Militarist or Gestalt Consciousness empires.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Galactic Paragons DLC.
Azaryn is a legendary paragon who will not contact Militarist or Gestalt Consciousness empires.

warnings: Azaryn will not spawn if the player empire is Militarist or Gestalt Consciousness.
curation.planner_notes: Avoid Militarist ethics or Gestalt Consciousness authority to allow Azaryn to appear. Once recruited, use the terraforming ability on three eligible planets.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect legendary paragon abilities or terraforming action counts.

source.name: Breaching the Planes
source.description: Explore an Astral Rift.
source.requirement: Explore an astral rift
source.hint: Play as an empire with the Riftworld origin.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Astral Planes DLC.
Requires entering and exploring at least one astral rift.

curation.planner_notes: The Riftworld origin (Astral Planes DLC) starts with an astral rift in the home system for immediate access.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track astral rift exploration counts.

source.name: Growing Planes
source.description: Explore 5 Astral Rifts in one game.
source.requirement: Explore 5 Astral Rifts
source.hint: Play as an empire with the Riftworld origin.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Astral Planes DLC.
Requires exploring five distinct astral rifts in a single game.

warnings: Astral rifts are finite; galaxy generation may limit availability.
curation.planner_notes: The Riftworld origin (Astral Planes DLC) ensures at least one rift in the home system. Active exploration with multiple science ships speeds up finding the remaining rifts.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track astral rift exploration counts.

source.name: Put a Cork in It
source.description: Construct a Dimensional Lock.
source.requirement: Use the Dimensional Lock action on an owned wormhole, Gateway or L-Gate
source.hint: Start a game with Wormhole Pairs set to 5x and play as an empire with the Riftworld origin.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Astral Planes DLC.
Requires access to at least one wormhole, Gateway, or L-Gate to target with the Dimensional Lock.

warnings: Dimensional Lock requires the Dimensional Locks astral action to be researched.
curation.planner_notes: Increase Wormhole Pairs galaxy setting for more wormholes. The Riftworld origin provides early astral rift access to help research astral actions faster.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect astral actions or Dimensional Lock usage.

source.name: My Name is Ozymandias
source.description: Claim the Eternal Throne.
source.requirement: Obtain the Eternal Throne relic
source.hint: Start a game with Tech/Tradition Cost and Mid-Game Start Year set to minimum and play as an empire with the Riftworld origin.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Astral Planes DLC.
Requires completing the astral rift chain that grants the Eternal Throne relic.

warnings: The Eternal Throne comes from a specific astral rift chain; exploration coverage is important.
curation.planner_notes: The Riftworld origin helps. Prioritize astral rift exploration to find the chain leading to the Eternal Throne.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect specific relic ownership or astral rift relic sources.

source.name: Returned to Form
source.description: make friends...
source.requirement: Obtain the legendary paragon zadigal
source.hint: Start a game with Tech/Tradition Cost and Mid-Game Start Year set to minimum and play as an empire with the Riftworld origin.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Astral Planes DLC.
Requires recruiting the legendary paragon Zadigal through astral rift exploration.

warnings: Zadigal's availability depends on astral rift exploration outcomes.
curation.planner_notes: The Riftworld origin helps by providing immediate astral rift access. Explore all available rifts to find Zadigal.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect specific legendary paragon recruitment.

source.name: Burning Brightly
source.description: Complete an Arc Furnace and a Dyson Swarm in the same system.
source.requirement: Have a fully-upgraded Arc Furnace and Dyson Swarm in the same system.
source.hint: The Arc Welders origin starts with an incomplete Arc Furnace.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Machine Age expansion.
Requires a completed Arc Furnace in a system.
Requires a completed Dyson Swarm in the same system as the Arc Furnace.

warnings: Both megastructures must be fully upgraded and in the exact same star system.
curation.planner_notes: The Arc Welders origin starts with an incomplete Arc Furnace, making this achievement significantly easier. Build the Dyson Swarm in the same system.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect completed megastructures, their upgrade stages, or co-location in the same system.

source.name: Mother Knows Best
source.description: Submit to all of Cetana's demands.
source.requirement: Declare war on Cetana after complying with all of her demands.
source.hint: Raiding Cetana's convoys and outposts does not count as disobeying a command.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires The Machine Age DLC.
Cetana must be the endgame crisis that spawns.

warnings: Cetana spawning as the crisis is RNG-dependent unless forced via galaxy settings.
Complying with all demands is time-sensitive; missed demands may lock the achievement.
Raiding convoys and outposts does not count as disobedience.
After complying, you must then declare war on Cetana.
curation.planner_notes: Force Cetana as the endgame crisis in galaxy settings if available. Follow all of Cetana's demands without exception (raiding is permitted). After all demands are met, declare war on Cetana to complete the achievement.
known_limitations: The current save parser cannot detect Cetana demand compliance state or the specific war declaration trigger.

source.name: Virtual Reality
source.description: Fully embrace the virtual world and leave no pop behind.
source.requirement: Complete the Virtuality tradition tree and have every pop in the empire have the Virtual trait.
source.hint: If the founder species has more than one template, only one of them will be given the Virtual trait.

conditions.notes: Must complete the Virtuality tradition tree (mutually exclusive with other Machine Age paths).
This achievement requires The Machine Age DLC.

warnings: If the founder species has multiple templates, only one template gets the Virtual trait. Must ensure all pops share the same template.
Gestalt Machine Intelligence authority likely required (Virtuality is a Machine tradition).
curation.planner_notes: Play as a Machine Intelligence and pursue the Virtuality tradition tree. Before completing it, ensure the founder species has only one template so the Virtual trait applies galaxy-wide.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect completed tradition trees or per-pop trait distributions.

source.name: Footsteps of the Prophet
source.description: As a Cybernetic Creed empire, defeat Cetana.
source.requirement: Defeat the Synthetic Queen crisis while playing as an empire with the Cybernetic Creed origin.
source.hint: Like all crises, lowering the Crisis Strength setting to 0.25x makes this a lot easier to achieve.

conditions.notes: Requires the Cybernetic Creed origin.
This achievement requires The Machine Age DLC.
Requires the Cetana (Synthetic Queen) endgame crisis.

warnings: Cetana must appear as the endgame crisis. This is guaranteed in The Machine Age if no other crisis is forced.
curation.planner_notes: Play as Cybernetic Creed origin. Cetana is the default crisis for The Machine Age content. Lower crisis strength to 0.25x for an easier fight.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads origin but does not yet identify Cetana as the active crisis or track her defeat.

source.name: Past the Expiration Date
source.description: As a nanite machine empire, consume a Fallen Empire's homeworld.
source.requirement: Used the Subsume World decision on a Fallen Empire homeworld until it becomes a Nanite World.
source.hint: Nanite swarm ships unlocked as a Nanotech empire are weak against Fallen Empire ships.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires The Machine Age DLC.
Must be a Machine Intelligence empire to access the Nanotech ascension path.
Must complete the Nanotech ascension path to unlock the Subsume World decision (mutually exclusive with other Machine Age paths).

warnings: Fallen Empire homeworlds are heavily fortified with FE-level fleets.
Nanite swarm ships are weak against Fallen Empire ships; a conventional fleet is recommended.
The Subsume World decision must be used repeatedly on the FE homeworld until fully converted.
curation.planner_notes: Start as a Machine Intelligence empire. Complete the Nanotech ascension path. Build a strong conventional fleet (nanite swarm ships are ineffective against FEs). Conquer or invade a Fallen Empire's homeworld, then use the Subsume World decision until the planet becomes a Nanite World.
known_limitations: The current save parser cannot detect ascension path completion or the Subsume World decision outcome.

source.name: A Universe of Paperclips
source.description: Create a perfect universe while meeting every quota as Obsessional Directive empire.
source.requirement: Win the game through the Cosmogenesis crisis path as a machine empire with the Obsessional Directive civic, while never failing to meet the quota.
source.hint: Play with Tech/Tradition Cost set to minimum.

conditions.notes: Requires the Obsessional Directive civic.
This achievement requires The Machine Age DLC.
Must complete the Cosmogenesis crisis path.

warnings: Never failing the Obsessional Directive quota is the hardest constraint — missed quotas invalidate the run.
Requires Machine Intelligence authority for the civic.
curation.planner_notes: Set Tech/Tradition Cost to minimum. Focus heavily on meeting the quota every cycle. Cosmogenesis path progresses the win condition.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads civics but does not yet track Obsessional Directive quota compliance or the Cosmogenesis situation.

source.name: Mediocre
source.description: Complete the Succumb to Tempest Challenge.
source.requirement: Complete the Succumb to Tempest objective of the Storm Fever situation.
source.hint: Obtained during the Storm Frenzy event chain (which requires the Storm Chasers origin).

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Cosmic Storms DLC.
The Storm Fever situation is triggered by the Storm Chasers origin.

warnings: The Storm Fever situation must be allowed to run and the 'Succumb to Tempest' objective must be selected.
curation.planner_notes: Play as the Storm Chasers origin and follow the Storm Fever situation to its completion, choosing the Succumb to Tempest path.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect Cosmic Storms situations or their objectives.

source.name: But at what cost
source.description: Kill Gurr.
source.requirement: Kill Gurr by getting rid of it after studying it.
source.hint: Gurr is found via the But They're Cute! anomaly, which can only spawn on habitable planets.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Cosmic Storms DLC.

warnings: Requires finding the 'But They're Cute!' anomaly, which can only spawn on habitable planets.
curation.planner_notes: Survey habitable planets extensively to find the anomaly. Complete the study chain and choose to eliminate Gurr afterwards.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect specific Cosmic Storms anomalies or Gurr-related events.

source.name: Humility before the Fall
source.description: Complete the Inetian Traders' Precursor chain.
source.requirement: Complete the Inetian Traders' Precursor chain.
source.hint: Enable only the Inetian Traders when starting a new game to avoid triggering another precursor chain.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Cosmic Storms DLC.
Requires completing the Inetian Traders precursor chain introduced in Cosmic Storms.

warnings: Precursor assignment is RNG-based unless other precursors are disabled.
curation.planner_notes: To force Inetian Traders, disable other precursors in galaxy settings. Then survey systems to find precursor archaeology sites.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect precursor identity or chain completion.

source.name: Unpopularity Contest
source.description: Use the Tempest Invocator relic to summon a storm.
source.requirement: Use the The Tempest Invocator relic.
source.hint: Getting the relic requires completing the adAkkaria Convention precursor story and deciding against freeing the storm.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Cosmic Storms DLC.
The Tempest Invocator relic is obtained by completing the adAkkaria Convention precursor story and choosing against freeing the storm.
Requires owning and activating the Tempest Invocator relic.

warnings: The adAkkaria Convention precursor must spawn. Choosing against freeing the storm is required to get the relic.
curation.planner_notes: Force the adAkkaria Convention precursor in galaxy settings. When completing the chain, choose the option that denies freeing the storm to obtain the Tempest Invocator.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect specific relic ownership or the adAkkaria Convention event chain outcomes.

source.name: Animal Farm
source.description: Reach 50 Vivarium capacity.
source.requirement: Reach 50 capacity on the Vivarium
source.hint: Capture space fauna and allow them to breed until the Vivarium reaches 50 capacity.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Grand Archive DLC.
Requires capturing and breeding enough space fauna to reach 50 Vivarium capacity.

curation.planner_notes: Capture space fauna using Gravitas Snares or Voidlures and allow them to breed in the Vivarium. Capacity grows with captured fauna population.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect Vivarium capacity levels.

source.name: It Belongs in a... oh right
source.description: Fill any Collection category with active Exhibits.
source.requirement: Fill any Collection category with active Exhibits
source.hint: 5 Xeno Geology artifacts can be obtained by completing the Habitable Worlds Survey event chain.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Grand Archive DLC.

warnings: Requires completing enough archaeological projects or events to fill a full collection category.
curation.planner_notes: The Habitable Worlds Survey event chain provides 5 Xeno Geology artifacts. Focus on completing archaeology sites and anomalies that yield collection exhibits.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track Grand Archive collection exhibit counts.

source.name: That's No Asteroid
source.description: Capture a Cutholoid with a Gravity Snare.
source.requirement: Capture a Cutholoid
source.hint: Cutholoids are hidden until a science ship surveys them.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Grand Archive DLC.
Cutholoids are hidden on celestial bodies and revealed when a science ship surveys them.

warnings: Cutholoids are not guaranteed to exist in a given galaxy.
curation.planner_notes: Survey all celestial bodies with science ships to reveal hidden Cutholoids. Once found, use a Gravity Snare to capture them.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect space fauna capture events.

source.name: Void Charmer
source.description: Build each type of Voidlure in one game, and attract each type of eligible Space Fauna.
source.requirement: Build each type of Voidlure and attract each type of Space Fauna
source.hint: Voidlures are unlocked by completing the Domestication tradition.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Grand Archive DLC.

warnings: Space fauna types present in the galaxy are RNG-dependent; not all types may be available.
curation.planner_notes: Complete the Domestication tradition tree to unlock all Voidlure types. Build each Voidlure type to attract the corresponding space fauna. Large galaxies with more systems increase fauna diversity.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet track Voidlure types built or space fauna attracted by type.

source.name: Wormageddon
source.description: Destroy a Voidworm nest.
source.requirement: Destroy a Voidworm nest
source.hint: Voidworm nests are found in certain black hole systems spread through the galaxy.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Grand Archive DLC.

warnings: Voidworm nests are found in certain black hole systems — black hole density affects availability.
curation.planner_notes: Explore black hole systems to find Voidworm nests. Destroy the nest with a fleet.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect Voidworm nest destruction events.

source.name: X Marks the Spot
source.description: Discover Captain Ness's Treasure Hoard.
source.requirement: Complete the 'Follow the Mysterious Chart' storyline
source.hint: This achievement is obtained as part of the Treasure Hunters origin story.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Grand Archive DLC.
This achievement requires the Treasure Hunters origin to trigger the 'Follow the Mysterious Chart' event chain.
Requires completing the special project chain from the Mysterious Chart.

warnings: Requires the Treasure Hunters origin (Grand Archive DLC).
curation.planner_notes: Start with the Treasure Hunters origin and follow the Mysterious Chart event chain to completion. The origin provides the chart in the home system.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect origins or specific special project completions.

source.name: Beastmaster
source.description: Defeat the endgame crisis without building a single artificial military ship.
source.requirement: Defeat an endgame crisis without building any military ships
source.hint: You can only use cloned space fauna. Start with a Beastmasters civic.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the Grand Archive DLC.
Requires the Beastmasters civic to clone space fauna.
Requires defeating any endgame crisis using only cloned space fauna.
Must not build any artificial military ships throughout the entire game.

warnings: Only cloned space fauna are permitted; building any corvette, destroyer, cruiser, battleship, Titan, or Juggernaut invalidates the run.
curation.planner_notes: Start with the Beastmasters civic, rely entirely on captured and cloned space fauna for military power. Lower crisis strength to 0.25x.
known_limitations: The current save parser does not yet detect ship build history or distinguish artificial vs. fauna ships.

source.name: I am the Invader Now
source.description: As a Starlit Citadel empire, control at least 5 systems connected to the Strange Portal network.
source.requirement: Control 5 systems connected to the Abandoned hatchery invaders system as an empire with the Starlit Citadel origin.
source.hint: The empire capital and the Abandoned Hatchery invaders systems both count for this achievement.

conditions.notes: Requires the Starlit Citadel origin.
This achievement requires the BioGenesis DLC.

warnings: Requires controlling systems on the Strange Portal network, which depends on galaxy generation.
curation.planner_notes: The empire capital and Abandoned Hatchery both count. Conquer 3 more systems connected to the portal network to reach 5.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads origin but does not yet track Strange Portal network system ownership.

source.name: Smörgåsblorg
source.description: As an Evolutionary Predators empire, eat six different phenotypes in one game.
source.requirement: Obtain DNA from six phenotypes as an empire with the Evolutionary Predators origin.
source.hint: Every time you finish the Adaptive Evolution situation, choose a different species to teach you.

conditions.notes: Requires the Evolutionary Predators origin.
This achievement requires the BioGenesis DLC.
Must collect DNA from 6 different species phenotypes via the Adaptive Evolution situation.

warnings: Requires enough diverse species in the galaxy to collect 6 different phenotypes.
curation.planner_notes: Each time the Adaptive Evolution situation completes, choose a different species for the DNA template.
known_limitations: The current save parser reads origin but does not yet track phenotype collection count.

source.name: Smugglers of Hope
source.description: Smuggle pops of your primary species out from an empire where they were enslaved or being purged.
source.requirement: Use the Smuggle Population operation to smuggle pops of the founder species from an empire where they were enslaved or being purged
source.hint: If playing as an empire with the Broken Shackles or Payback origin, Minamar Specialized Industries will have pops of the founder species enslaved.

conditions.notes: This achievement requires the BioGenesis DLC.
Must successfully complete at least one Smuggle Population espionage operation targeting a specific founder-species pop.

warnings: Requires another empire to have pops of your founder species in slavery or being purged.
Broken Shackles and Payback origins have guaranteed enslaved founder pops at Minamar Specialized Industries.
Requires building a Spy Network on the target empire to unlock the Smuggle Population operation.
If the target empire fully purges the founder pops before the operation completes, the opportunity is lost.
curation.planner_notes: Play as Broken Shackles or Payback origin for a guaranteed target. Build a Spy Network on Minamar Specialized Industries. Use the Smuggle Population operation when available. The operation extracts founder species pops from slavery.
known_limitations: The current save parser cannot detect specific espionage operation outcomes or pop-slavery status at target empires.

source.name: Born to be Wild
source.description: As a Wilderness empire, restore all of the Hive Fallen Empire's worlds to your natural state.
source.requirement: Capture and then terraform all Shattered Fragments worlds while playing as an empire with the Wilderness origin.
source.hint: Use the zero empires trick and set the end-game date as late as possible.

conditions.notes: Requires the Wilderness origin.
This achievement requires the BioGenesis DLC.

warnings: Hive Fallen Empire (Shattered Fragments worlds) must be present in the galaxy.
All Shattered Fragments worlds must be captured and terraformed back to natural state.
